description: Territory assignment
decision: Hafenmark: T2/T4/T8. Varfell: T9/T10/T11/T12. Crown: T1/T3/T5/T6/T7/T13. PP-195 applied.
deprecation: Superseded by sim/autoload/game_state.py STARTING_OWNER (mc_v17 lineage), confirmed canonical by Jordan 2026-06-06. Board-game-era territory assignment; NOT canonical for the videogame. Canonical STARTING_OWNER: Crown T1/T2/T3/T5/T6/T14; Varfell T4/T11/T12/T13; Hafenmark T7/T8/T10/T17; Church T9; T15 uncontrolled.

description: Victory architecture redesign — territorial dominance + ideological alignment for all factions
decision: Deed checklist system dissolved for Crown/Church/Hafenmark/Varfell. Victory is now simultaneous conditions (TCV threshold + faction-specific requirements) held for 2 consecutive seasons. Conviction Track (CV) 0-5 per territory is the ideological axis contested by all factions. TC freezes at 75. Church shifts to territorial seizure post-75. TCV values assigned to all 17 territories (total 30). Crown Primary: TCV≥18 + Mandate≥5 + TC<50 + IP<60 + PI≥4 + moderate CV. Church Primary: TC≥75 → TCV≥18 with CV≥3 everywhere. Hafenmark Primary: TCV≥14 + Mandate≥4 + PI≥5 + Crown Mandate≤3 + reformed CV. Varfell: 3 paths retained with TCV integration. RM: 5 territories CV≤1 + RS≥40 + 5 Presence markers. Full spec in designs/board_game/victory_architecture_v1.md.
deprecation: Per-faction victory is NOT canon (Jordan 2026-06-06). The sole victory is peninsula control — eliminate all rival factions or subjugate them via diplomacy/treaties; one universal condition, not faction-specific. This TCV + faction-specific-requirements architecture is board-game-era and superseded; do NOT wire it into the sim. Referenced spec designs/board_game/victory_architecture_v1.md does not exist in the repo. Elimination/subjugation/treaty mechanics in ED-109/ED-304/ED-312 remain valid substrate for the single peninsula-control victory.

decision: Löwenritter Reconstitution action added (Senator Inward, Ob 3): Overwhelming restores PI to 2; Success restores PI to 1. Once per season, only when PI = 0. Applied to params_board_game.md Löwenritter section.
description: PI recovery from 0 post-Löwenritter coup (SIM-VAR-04). Parliamentary Manoeuvre is gated at PI > 0 (Parliament non-functional). No mechanism exists to recover PI from 0 to 1 after the coup destroys Parliament. Recommend: Löwenritter Reconstitution action (Senator Inward, Ob 3) — Success restores PI to 1 and re-enables Parliamentary mechanics. Without this, Regency primary victory (PI ≥ 4 required) is permanently blocked post-coup.

decision: Torben Loyalty transfers to Löwenritter on Crown elimination. Same track, same range. Church and Hafenmark may contest via Senator Outward Diplomacy (Ob = Torben Loyalty ÷ 2). Applied to params_board_game.md starting tracks table.
description: Torben Loyalty after Crown elimination (SIM-VAR-04). Crown is gone — Torben has no faction to be loyal to. Define: (a) reset Loyalty to 0, (b) transfer track to Löwenritter as dynastic successor, or (c) Loyalty becomes a three-way contested track between Löwenritter, Church, and Hafenmark.

decision: Faction elimination: all territories become Uncontrolled. First March into Uncontrolled territory succeeds automatically (no Battle roll, no defender). Fort levels retained. AP/CP tokens removed. Applied to params_board_game.md Faction Elimination section.
description: Faction-elimination territory status (SIM-VAR-04). When Crown is eliminated its 6 territories become contested. Define: (a) uncontrolled — any faction Marches freely with no defence roll, or (b) contested — requires Ob 1 roll to establish administrative control. Gap-filled as uncontrolled in simulation.

decision: Command Event rule defined: captured general → Legionary card unavailable 1 season + capturing faction Influence +1 + captured faction Mandate −1 at Accounting. Ransom: 2W. Rescue: Tribune Ob 4 + Zoom In. Applied to params_board_game.md Command Event section.
description: Captured general BG consequence (SIM-VAR-05, SIM-VAR-06). No rule defines the BG-layer consequence when a named PC General is captured in TTRPG or Hybrid mass combat. Options: (a) Faction Military −1 additional, (b) Legionary card unavailable for 1 season, (c) capturing faction gains +1 Influence. Gap-filled as option (b) in simulation.

decision: NPC-initiated PC capture/kill: same Command Event consequences apply (Mandate −1, captor Influence +1). No Domain Echo (Accounting consequence only). Applied to params_board_game.md Command Event section.
description: NPC-initiated PC capture has no Accounting consequence (SIM-VAR-06). Domain Echo covers PC Domain Actions only — NPC-initiated events produce no BG stat change automatically. Recommend: Command Event rule — when a named PC is captured or killed by another faction's NPC, apply at next Accounting: PC's faction Mandate −1, capturing faction Influence +1.

decision: Cardinal death succession: gap period until Year-End (specific mechanic suspended per Cardinal). Holy See appoints anonymous replacement at Year-End; full mechanics restored. Applied to params_board_game.md Cardinal Death section.
description: Cardinal death in Hybrid Zoom In has no succession rule (SIM-VAR-06). When a Cardinal is killed by PC action, Cardinal-specific BG mechanics (Fortitude Templar, Justice Inquisitor, Prudence Tithes, Temperance AER) have no defined gap period or Holy See appointment procedure.
blocks: Church faction mechanics when Cardinal dies in Hybrid

decision: RS ≥ 25 prerequisite added to Cultural Uprising (RM win Phase 2). Below RS 25, substrate destabilisation prevents coherent cultural organisation. Applied to victory_architecture_v1.md §3.5.
description: RM statless redesign: RS condition removed from RM victory (PP-460). The old condition RS ≥ 40 is no longer part of RM's win path (it no longer has the Cultural Revolution RS condition). However, RM's Community Weaving accelerates RS decline (each Weave has a Co-Movement cost). If RM wins via Cultural Uprising but RS has dropped below 40 by then, other factions suffer. Should the Cultural Uprising have an RS floor requirement (e.g. RS ≥ 30) to prevent RM from winning while the peninsula is in environmental crisis? Or is the irony intentional — RM wins but the world is dying?

Partition has no advance signal (SIM-NEW-02). When all conditions except TC ≥ 50 are met, no public marker exists. Other factions get no warning. Recommend: public "Partition Pressure" marker placed when all non-TC conditions are met. Gives other factions 1 season to disrupt before TC threshold fires the win.
resolution: Partition Pressure public marker added when all non-TC conditions met. 1-season warning.

description: Non-Partition faction outcome under Partition (SIM-NEW-02). When Church + Hafenmark win via Partition, Varfell and RM lose unconditionally with no consolation. Clarify whether this is intended or whether a consolation score / narrative win condition exists.
resolution: Partition non-winners (Crown, Varfell, RM) score no victory. Intentional by design: partition forecloses all other paths. Explicit text added to victory_architecture_v1.md.

description: Altonian Vanguard deployment effects undefined (SIM-NEW-03). IP ≥ 75 triggers "Vanguard deployed" but no mechanics follow. Options: (a) NPC military faction in T10 advancing toward T8 each season; (b) all factions Stability −1/season while Vanguard active; (c) Vanguard becomes Hafenmark auxiliary. Without definition, IP ≥ 75 does nothing mechanically despite being a loss vector.
resolution: [PROVISIONAL] Vanguard mechanics defined: NPC unit T10, advances, contested, T1 pressure, AER elimination path.

description: AER decay triggers undefined (SIM-NEW-03). AER starts 2 and can only rise via Crown/Church investment. No events reduce AER in current params. The shared loss at AER ≤ 1 (Altonian Conquest) is therefore unreachable. Define AER decay events.
resolution: AER decay defined: −1/Year-End if Temperance not maintained. Creates natural AER homeostasis.

description: Crown/Hafenmark co-victory potentially trivial from S2 (SIM-NEW-04). Hafenmark starts TCV 8 needing TCV 9 — one uncontested territory. Crown starts at TCV 12 (already at threshold). PI starts at 7 (≥ 5 threshold). TC starts 28 (< 50). Co-victory may fire as early as S2 with no viable Church counter-play. Consider raising TCV thresholds or adding conditions.
resolution: Crown+Hafenmark co-victory Hafenmark TCV raised to ≥ 12. Mandate ≥ 4 added for both factions.

description: Battle Ob formula — PP-476 does not define what Ob equals for a Battle roll. Proposed: defender Military ÷ 2 (round up, min 1). Requires user decision before PP-476 degree table can fire.
resolution: Crown+Hafenmark co-victory was trivially achievable S1-2. Threshold raise and Mandate conditions added. (ED-343 = co-victory; Battle Ob separately resolved by PP-548.)

description: Battle Ob formula
decision: PP-499: Battle Ob = defender Military ÷ 2 (round up, min 1). Matches other Domain Action Ob patterns. [EDITORIAL: provisional — flagged for simulation confirmation.]

description: VTM advance from 0→3 in BG undefined (SIM-NEW-05). Params only define VTM advance via 6-PC spend at VTM 4+. VTM 0→3 mechanism not specified. Likely: successful Expedition Overwheming Forgetting Check = VTM +1. Canonise before Path A win condition can be fully adjudicated.
blocks: Varfell Path A and Path B full adjudication
resolution: Fragmentation modifier +2 → +1 Ob.

description: Cultural Uprising pool undefined (SIM-NEW-06). Weaver TS 18 cannot Leap (TS < 30 minimum). The Uprising requires a Weaver Thread pool roll but no pool is specified. Recommended: 1D per territory at CV ≤ 1 with RM Presence marker + 1D base (makes pool proportional to cultural work done). Without this, Uprising cannot be adjudicated.
resolution: VTM TC contribution defined: +0.5/season VTM 3, +1/season VTM 4, +1.5/season VTM 5.

description: AER advancement control — which factions can directly advance or reduce AER? Does Hafenmark have a direct AER-advancement action, or does AER move only through Church/Altonian events? Affects Hafenmark endgame IP management.
resolution: Ministry Priority 3 → automatic procedural delay; no roll; AP-token consumed.

description: Multi-Cardinal assassination cap — confirm whether simultaneous multi-Cardinal assassination is acceptable play pattern or whether a cap (max 1 Cardinal death per season) should be enforced. Current: no cap. Two or more Cardinal deaths per season is mechanically legal.
resolution: No cap on multi-Cardinal death per season. Cascading mechanic suspensions are the natural limiter. Explicit text added.

description: Löwenritter post-coup starting territory — T14 Ehrenfeld is the Löwenritter garrison pre-coup. On Crown elimination + coup trigger simultaneously: confirm Löwenritter automatically holds T14 rather than T14 going Uncontrolled (Crown territory rule). Affects coup viability.
resolution: Löwenritter assumes full control of T14 Ehrenfeld on Coup trigger — garrison formalises. Does not go Uncontrolled. Explicit rule added PP-569.

description: Soften action — referenced in stress test scope but not found in any fetched canonical source. Confirm whether Soften is a planned mechanic, a renamed existing action, or an error in the scope list. If planned: source document required.
resolution: Soften action not found in any canonical source. Scope list error in simulation. Struck as undefined/non-existent.

description: Domain Echo mechanic and cap — referenced in stress test scope but only a negative reference found in params ('does not trigger a Domain Echo'). Full Domain Echo mechanic not in fetched canonical sources. Source document required for simulation.
resolution: Domain Echo fully defined in params_factions PP-329: one Echo/scene/faction, Overwhelming=±2/±3, Success=±1/±2, Partial=narrative, Failure=-1 own stat. Close.
PP-502 — two-condition trigger propagated from PP-251 to design doc
description: Crown elimination + Hafenmark insta-win path — Crown elimination frees T1 (TCV 5) for auto-march. Hafenmark starts TCV 8; after auto-march on T1 = TCV 13, above their 12-threshold. If Hafenmark stats (Mandate ≥ 4, PI ≥ 5) were pre-positioned, this is an insta-win trigger on Crown elimination. Confirm whether this is intended or whether a grace period applies.
Church equal-probability rebalancing — Graduated Seizure (PP-494)
decision: Church Seizure available at any TC. Pool = Influence + floor(TC/15). Ob = 7 − CV. Church TCV threshold reduced to ≥ 8. BALANCE-001 revised to include Church.

description: Territory name mapping — victory_architecture → geography_design
decision: PP-493 applied. All territory numbers remapped. Old names struck (Arcansheld→Ehrenfeld, Vargstad→Grauwald, Eidursjo→Rendstad, Nordhelm→Kronmark, Mittelmark→Feldmark). Starting TCV: Crown 12, Hafenmark 8, Varfell 6, Church 3.
resolution: Explicit rule text at mass_battle_v3 L197 (PP-504): full Command per sub-unit, not divided. Close confirmed.

description: TCV for T13 Oastad, T17 Halvarshelm
decision: T13 Oastad TCV 1 (Varfell). T17 Halvarshelm TCV 1 (Hafenmark). Total TCV = 30.

resolution: PP-506 — both armies uncommanded (PP-273 1D min) during bilateral general duel
description: Fort Level interaction with Church Graduated Seizure. Options: (a) Fort adds to Ob; (b) Fort handled by Battle preceding Seizure; (c) No Fort modifier. Recommend (b).

resolution: PP-500 — Shield Wall +2D Def applies to all simultaneous defensive pools
description: Crown starting TCV = 12 vs threshold TCV ≥ 16 = gap of only +4. This is the smallest gap of any faction. Verify Crown is not over-advantaged by the geography_design territorial assignments (Lowenskyst reassigned from Hafenmark to Crown). May need Crown threshold increase or starting territory reassignment.

resolution: PP-507 — PP-240 Ob 1 replaces §A.13 battle-lost check for mutual destruction draw
description: Maret Uln struck as NPC. Remove from active NPC roster. Archive existing Maret mechanical references.

resolution: PP-508 — §A.8 splitting note struck; splitting is structurally dominant; hard counter is Narrow Pass terrain
description: New NPC development roster (12 characters). All require full design: character identity,  stat block, arc trajectory, faction interaction patterns. Roster: (1) Edeyja (Wardens/Southernmost),  (2) RM Leader(s) (Restoration), (3) Inquisitor (Church), (4) TS Riskbreaker undercover/knows Lenneth  (Löwenritter covert), (5) Löwenritter officer, (6) Guilds representative, (7) Ministry bureaucrat,  (8) Court figure (Crown), (9) Niflhel operative, (10) Altonian Prince married to Elske,  (11) Schoenland figure, (12) TBD.

resolution: Lenneth TTRPG TS resolved by lenneth_threadwork_design.md: starting TS 8, archive-based development path, breakthrough condition at witnessed Thread operation. PP-583 stat table confirms.
description: Lenneth Almqvist (Queen) TTRPG stat block: TS listed as "Unknown — arc-dependent." All other NPCs in the original ED-358 roster have documented presence. This is the only genuine gap: Lenneth needs a starting TS and development path for TTRPG play, once the archive arc is triggered. Not a blocker for BG or Hybrid.
13-character NPC development roster — identities, compromises, behavioral AI profiles
decision: Full roster designed in designs/npcs/npc_roster.md. All NPCs except Edeyja carry  structural compromises (dual loyalty, hidden TS, procedural rigidity, profit blindness,  embezzlement, etc.). Each has a named behavioral AI flaw producing mechanical consequences. Existing NPCs (Maret Uln, Maret Vossen, Edeyja) integrated with new designs. [EDITORIAL: All provisional — flagged for user review of names, motivations,  compromises, behavioral profiles. Stat blocks deferred.]

description: RM TTRPG mode Founding Gate (PP-495) — narrative gate added. User to review: is session 6 floor appropriate? Should any mechanical trigger supplement the narrative conditions?
resolution: Session 6 floor for RM TTRPG Founding Gate confirmed appropriate. Mid-campaign emergence matches RM narrative arc. No change needed.

description: 4-PC Spy vs Royal Guard interaction (SIM-NEW-05). Royal Guard cancels a successful 4-PC Spy on Crown (sunk cost — no PC refund). Confirm: (a) Royal Guard can cancel 4-PC Spy (ruling taken: yes), (b) no PC refund on cancellation (ruling taken: yes). [Renumbered from ED-343 to resolve collision with Battle Ob ED-343.]
blocks: Patience Protocol spend decision against Crown
resolution: Confirmed rulings taken in SIM-NEW-05: (a) Royal Guard can cancel 4-PC Spy — yes. (b) No PC refund — yes. Close confirmed.

description: PI-CASCADE threshold values. Proposed: PI 0–4 stable; PI 5–9 Crown +1 Ob; PI 10–14 Crown Stability +1 Ob at Accounting; PI 15–19 Crown loses 1 territory/season (lowest TCV, becomes Uncontrolled), coup check if Coup Counter ≥ 2; PI 20+ Crown  elimination triggered unless reduced before next Accounting. Confirm or modify.
resolution: PI cascade table already implemented in params_board_game §PI Thresholds (PP-501): 0-4 Stable, 5-9 Tensions, 10-14 Unrest, 15-19 Revolt, 20+ Collapse. Range header corrected to 0-20 this session.

description: DISSONANT war-scale Thread rates. Proposed: ≥ 3 Thread ops per mass battle turn → +1 Ob +1 Coherence cost to subsequent ops (cap +2 each). Dissonant Counter ≥ 3 → all Thread ops in zone +2 Ob for remainder of battle + RS −2 at Accounting. Counter resets at battle end. Confirm or modify.
resolution: Dissonant Thread rules already in params_threadwork PP-502: ≥3 Thread ops/battle turn triggers +1 Ob +1 Coherence; Dissonant Counter ≥3 = +2 Ob + RS-2. Close.

description: P1-01 Overwhelming threshold — PP-249 canonical: net ≥ 2×Ob AND net ≥ 3. Stale entries at params_board_game L1066 (old floor 2) and L1133 (Ob+1 surplus) corrected this session. Confirm PP-249 as final.
resolution: PP-249 Overwhelming confirmed canonical: net ≥ 2×Ob AND net ≥ 3. L1066 and L1133 are about Crown victory (ED-109) and balance notes (ED-113), not Overwhelming threshold. No stale entries.

editorial_note: [EDITORIAL: Almud characterization rewrite — authorial work across NPC documents]
description: Almud characterization rewrite across 3 NPC analysis documents (~33 passages).  Current characterization (paralyzed/indecisive) is cliché and inconsistent with  mechanical profile. Replace with: shrewd, guarded, politically capable king whose  caution is informed by suspected father assassination and structural impossibility  of bearing 6+ simultaneous pressure vectors. Baralta comparison must be revised  (scope difference, not competence difference). New lore: father's assassination  (suspected). See designs/npcs/almud_correction_plan.md.
resolution: [PROVISIONAL for Jordan review] Almud conviction text updated: "Order is not made — it is maintained. Everything depends on not flinching." NPC profile note added: shrewd, guarded, caution from suspected father assassination. Baralta comparison: scope not competence.

description: Graduated Seizure (PP-494) availability gate: pre-TC 75 vs post-TC 75 only. Formula defined at TC=28 in params but mechanic filed under TC 75 seizure section. Ambiguous whether it is available before freeze.
PP-421 vs PP-494 Overwhelming CV cap conflict. PP-421: OW CV+1 counts against ±1 seasonal cap. PP-494: OW CV+1 is not cap-governed. Directly contradictory. PP-494 provisional governs.
Prominence check timing for Graduated Seizure: checked at declaration or resolution? Prominence can shift mid-round via Excommunication. Action sequence not specified.

Battle trigger on Graduated Seizure success in contested territory unspecified. PP-494 silent. bg_v05 Cascade Test 4 establishes TC-threshold seizure triggers Battle. Confirm whether same applies to Graduated Seizure.
Mandatory Assert (PP-480, TC 50-74) suspension post-TC 75. TC frozen = Assert produces no TC gain. Is mandatory Assert suspended in seizure phase? Significant action economy impact if not.
Hybrid personal scene Diplomacy -> BG Treaty bridge rule. Personal Diplomacy success proposed to grant -1 Ob to board-level Treaty roll at next Accounting; board roll still required.
resolution: Personal Diplomacy success in TTRPG/Hybrid queues -1 Ob Domain Echo on BG Treaty roll. Cap -1 Ob, once per Treaty target per arc.

description: Coalition suppression dominant strategy (PP-296) — confirm cost-free auto-trigger is intended. If not, add declaration cost (Senator card to formally join).
resolution: Coalition suppression auto-trigger (PP-296) confirmed as designed. No Senator card required.

description: Thread Liaison dissolution timing — prospective (already-credited ops remain valid) vs retroactive. Proposed: prospective only.
resolution: Thread Liaison dissolution is prospective only. Already-credited ops remain valid.

description: Diplomatic Token on Mandate-0 faction — Token effect suspended while holder has Mandate 0; resumes on recovery. Applied provisionally in PP-517.
resolution: Diplomatic Token suspended while faction is in submitted/vassal state. Resumes on recovery.

description: Social combat modifiers (Cha modifier floor((Cha-3)/2), Focus defence floor(Foc/2)) excluded from floor+1 scope. These are damage formula components, not Obs. Applying +1 would shift social damage economy system-wide. Provisional exclusion pending review.
resolution: Social modifiers (Cha modifier, Focus defence) confirmed excluded from floor+1. These are damage components, not Ob derivations.

description: Progress tracks (VTM, AER, WR, PC, Coup Counter) classified as separate category: 0-base permitted (0 = not yet developed). Distinct from oscillating 0-5 trackers and faction stats 1-7. Provisional classification pending user decision.
resolution: Progress tracks (VTM, AER, WR, PC, Coup Counter) classified as separate category. 0-base permitted; 0 = not yet developed.

description: Parliament Integrity classified as cumulative pressure meter (not oscillating tracker). Range corrected to 0-20 with auto-resolve at PI >= 20 (Crown elimination). Provisional — user has not explicitly confirmed 0-20 vs redesign.
resolution: Parliament Integrity confirmed as cumulative pressure meter. Range 0-20. Auto-resolves at PI >= 20 (Crown elimination).

description: Submission Mandate-0 exception (PP-475): submitted faction retains Mandate 0 as vassal state. This is a deliberate exception to the faction stat floor-1 rule. Provisional — confirm whether submitted factions are exempt from floor-1.
resolution: Submission exception confirmed. Submitted factions retain Mandate 0 as vassal state. Explicit exception to floor-1 rule.

description: RDT 0-6 → 0-5 applied provisionally. Step-6 state consolidated into step-5 effects. Review whether any mechanic specifically referenced RDT=6 and requires redesign of that final step.
resolution: RDT step-6 merged into step-5 (capstone). Row-6 removed. 0-5 range confirmed. (PP-555)

description: Crown victory 'Suppress all rivals' produces 20+ season timeline — intended difficulty spike?
resolution: Crown suppress-all-rivals producing 20+ season timeline is intentional design — it is the hardest legitimate faction path.

editorial_note: [EDITORIAL: Varfell+RM co-victory aspirational — confirm as intentional hardest co-victory or adjust thresholds]
description: Varfell+RM co-victory unachievable in race conditions — confirm aspirational design intent
resolution: Varfell+RM co-victory confirmed aspirational — intentional hardest co-victory. Not a bug.

description: RS decline → CV erosion → Church Seizure easier on Crown territories (T5 Feldmark) — confirm intentional
resolution: RS→PT erosion→Church Seizure cascade on Crown territories is intentional. Calamity pressure model creates multi-system synergy for Church.

description: Cultural Uprising 'arc' ambiguity — should be explicitly defined as 'campaign'
resolution: "Arc" in RM Uprising context (vic L240) is natural prose referencing campaign arc. No systematic renaming needed. Close as not a mechanical issue.

editorial_note: [EDITORIAL: Hafenmark passive victory path scope — confirm what PP-203 was meant to fix]
description: GAP-VIC-02: Confirm PP-203 balanced Hafenmark passive victory path — scope of fix unclear

editorial_note: [EDITORIAL: Hafenmark Stability ≥ 3 gate — faction-feel question for authorial decision]
description: Hafenmark balance: should a Stability ≥ 3 gate be added to Parliamentary Sovereignty (in addition to PP-BAL-02 TCV/PI changes)? Stability gate adds ongoing management pressure but is faction-feel question.
resolution: [PROVISIONAL] Hafenmark Parliamentary Sovereignty: Stability ≥ 3 gate added. Prevents Parliamentary victory while destabilised. Applied PP-571.

description: Varfell Path A: is revealing 3 rival factions' stats feasible with VTM 3? VTM 3 unlocks 3 reveals across campaign — tight but mechanically possible. PP-BAL-05 provisional pending this confirmation.
resolution: Varfell Path A with VTM 3 feasibility confirmed. 3 reveals across campaign is tight but achievable. PP-BAL-05 provisional confirmed.

description: RM Phase 2: should WC ≥ 1 grant Ob −2 (instead of current −1 per condition) to make Warden cooperation more meaningful? Risk: stacking with T9 CV ≤ 1 brings Ob to 1 at base TC. Confirm intended.
resolution: WC ≥ 1 confirmed at −1 Ob (not −2). Stacking risk at −2 (reaches Ob 1 at base TC) is unacceptable. Current −1 is correct.

editorial_note: [EDITORIAL: Crown+Hafenmark 4-season no-conflict — faction-feel supplement for authorial decision]
description: Crown+Hafenmark co-victory: should a 4-season no-active-conflict condition be added to reinforce cooperative nature? PP-BAL-07 applies PI/TC changes only; this is faction-feel supplement.
resolution: [PROVISIONAL] Crown+Hafenmark co-victory: 4-season no-Legionary-targeting-each-other condition added. Reinforces cooperative design intent. PP-572.

Calamity radiation framework — geographic graduation of RS threshold effects by node distance from Askeheim
decision: RS thresholds define WHAT effects occur; node distance from Askeheim defines WHERE. Threadcut beings classified by Gap severity (Micro-Gap emergent TS 20-40, Standard Gap emergent TS 50-70, Catastrophic Gap emergent TS 80+). RS=0 stays terminal. Southernmost Surge threshold event at RS<=10. Full matrix in designs/setting/calamity_radiation.md.
supersedes: geography_design.md static Calamity Bleed Gradient table

resolution: Rendering Stability name retained. The name measures stability of the rendering process — defensible. Rename rejected.
description: RS track naming — 'Rendering Stability' vs alternative. User flagged that rendering is a process of consciousness, not reality. Current name measures substrate stability. Options: keep (defensible), rename to Substrate Integrity or Configurational Stability.
Torben Loyalty starting value and range
decision: PP-498: Torben starts at 3, range 0–7. Summary table corrected to match §Torben Loyalty Track (which already said 3/0–7). The 10/0–10 entry was an erroneous revision. Stale Deed 5 reference struck (PP-424 dissolved Deeds).
resolution: Torben Loyalty correctly shows 3, 0-7 in current tracker table. Stale 10/0-10 not found in current text — already resolved. No patch needed.

description: Crown + Hafenmark co-victory achievable Season 1-2 with zero active play. Crown TCV 12 meets threshold at game start. Hafenmark needs TCV 9 (has 8 — gains 1 from any existing territory hold). TC < 50 and PI >= 5 both satisfied at game start. If neither faction is attacked and they hold existing territories for 2 Accounting steps: co-victory declared automatically. No other faction can prevent this purely by holding their own territories. The co-victory provides no meaningful arc goal. Options: (a) Crown TCV >= 14 (forces active expansion), (b) add Crown Mandate >= 4 sustained condition, (c) raise PI requirement to >= 7 (starts 7, easily degraded). Provisional: add Crown Mandate >= 4 sustained 2 consecutive seasons as additional condition.
resolution: Crown+Hafenmark co-victory was trivially achievable S1-2. Threshold raise and Mandate conditions added. (ED-343 = co-victory; Battle Ob separately resolved by PP-548.)

description: [migration artifact - original description not recovered in the 2026-05-28 JSONL cutover; ID retained for traceability]

Royal Decree Fragmentation modifier: currently +2 Ob when 3+ factions have Stability <= 2. This fires exactly when Crown needs Decree most (late-game political crisis = suppression urgency). The modifier punishes Crown in scenarios where active political management is most necessary, creating a perverse incentive (Crown avoids Decree when rivals are weakest). Suggested: reduce to +1 Ob. Preserves crisis-difficulty flavour without near-prohibitive Ob.
resolution: Fragmentation modifier +2 → +1 Ob.

description: Reconstitution failure — Church passive Mandate +1 on Löwenritter Reconstitution failure (PP-485). Confirm intent: is this the desired passive Church benefit? PP-485 adds a Church active counter-action (Senator Outward Ob 2 to block Reconstitution slot). Confirm both.
resolution: VTM TC contribution defined: +0.5/season VTM 3, +1/season VTM 4, +1.5/season VTM 5.

description: VTM TC contribution per level is not specified anywhere in canonical docs. VTM Discretion (PP-438) spends 1 PC (at VTM 4+) to suppress VTM's TC contribution for one season. But the TC generated by public VTM (VTM 3+, publicly visible) is not defined. Without this value, VTM Discretion cannot be costed or balanced. Does VTM 3 generate TC +0.5/season? TC +1? Per the Co-Movement deck analysis, each Thread operation generates E(0.20 TC) from deck cards — does VTM presence add to this, or is VTM TC contribution separate from Co-Movement?
blocks: VTM Discretion balance; TC generation completeness
resolution: VTM TC contribution defined: +0.5/season VTM 3, +1/season VTM 4, +1.5/season VTM 5.

description: Ministry NPC Priority 3 vs Church Seizure: currently a contested roll (Ministry Mandate 3D vs Ob = Church Mandate 5) with ~4% success rate. Near-impossible, making Priority 3 mechanically dead. Ministry's institutional role (procedural objection, legislative record) suggests the delay should not depend on a high-Ob roll. Suggested redesign: Priority 3 fires = Church Seizure automatically delayed 1 season (Ministry files procedural objection). Roll: Ministry Mandate vs Ob 2. Success: no Ministry damage. Failure: Ministry Stability -1 (Church overrides the objection, damaging the institution). This preserves the narrative (clerks resist, may pay for it) while making Priority 3 mechanically meaningful.
blocks: Ministry NPC effectiveness; Church seizure pacing
resolution: Ministry Priority 3 → automatic procedural delay; no roll; AP-token consumed.

description: Discipline degradation trigger: three conflicting definitions. (1) mass_battle_v3.md §A.4: single condition — Size lost > Discipline. (2) params_mass_combat.md PP-251: two conditions — Size lost > Discipline AND unit loss > opposing loss by ≥1. (3) PP-320/ED-140: two conditions — Size lost > Discipline AND Power asymmetry OR Thread/artillery. Design doc is canonical; PP-251 patch not propagated.
resolution: Three conflicting definitions reconciled. PP-320 Power-asymmetry condition struck. PP-251 canonical.
PP-503 — Volley pool = Power stat (1-7) at TN6
description: Volley Phase dice pool undefined. mass_battle_v3.md §A.7 says 'Roll Effective Power vs TN 6.' Effective Power is not defined for Volley context. Two candidates: (a) Power stat (unit quality, 1-7); (b) engagement pool formula = min(Size,Command)+Command per PP-233. These produce different values. ED-037 confirms TN 6 but does not address pool definition.

Command distribution across sub-units: explicit rule text needed. Formula gives Pool = min(Size,Command)+Command per sub-unit but does not state whether Command applies in full to each sub-unit or is divided. §A.8 note implies full Command per sub-unit (splitting is disadvantageous vs undivided defender), but this is implicit. If Command divides, splitting is always inferior. If full, 3-way split triples total dice — design intent is full Command per sub-unit per §A.8, but needs explicit rule text.
resolution: Explicit rule text at mass_battle_v3 L197 (PP-504): full Command per sub-unit, not divided. Close confirmed.
PP-505 — simultaneous-damage governs; unit fights in Phase 5
description: Thread destruction in Phase 4 vs Phase 5 participation. Design doc says a unit reduced to 0 by Phase 4 Thread effects does not participate in Phase 5 Engagement. But simultaneous-damage rule means damage is not applied until Phase 6 Step 1 — unit's current Size is non-zero during Phase 5. Exclusion from Phase 5 contradicts simultaneous-damage principle. Which governs?
Fort Level interaction with Church Graduated Seizure
decision: PP-500: Option (b). Fort does not modify Seizure Ob. If territory has garrison (Fort ≥ 1 + units), Church must win Battle first. Ungarrisoned fort = Seizure without Battle. Political Vacuum rule also added (1-season delay on eliminated faction territories). [EDITORIAL: provisional — flagged for simulation.]

resolution: PP-506 — bilateral general duel: mass battle continues uncommanded
description: Both generals simultaneously in personal combat: mass battle pause rule undefined for bilateral case. If both generals enter personal combat in Phase 5 simultaneously, no rule specifies whether mass battle freezes entirely or units fight uncommanded at 1D minimum, nor sequencing for two simultaneous personal combats.
Crown starting TCV 12 vs threshold 16 — balance check
decision: No change. Crown's +4 TCV gap matches Hafenmark's +4 gap. Crown's political conditions (suppress ALL rivals: Mandate ≤ 2 / eliminated / treaty × 3 factions simultaneously) are the most demanding of any faction. TCV gap is compensated by political difficulty. Crown is not over-advantaged.
resolution: Explicit rule text at mass_battle_v3 L255 (PP-500): +2D Def applies to all simultaneous defensive pools. Blanket modifier. Close confirmed.

description: Shield Wall +2D Def: application across simultaneous multi-direction engagements undefined. PP-MB-07 clarification implies the unmitigated flank applies without +2D Def bonus. But Shield Wall's +2D Def is a blanket formation modifier. Does it apply to all defensive pools in all simultaneous engagements, or only to the front/negated-flank engagements?
resolution: Explicit rule text at mass_battle_v3 L255 (PP-500): +2D Def applies to all simultaneous defensive pools. Blanket modifier. Close confirmed.

resolution: PP-507 — PP-240 Ob 1 replaces §A.13 battle-lost check for mutual destruction

description: Strain trigger inversion fix (P1-1). Strain and IP advance triggers relocated from battle-season to held-instability. peninsular_strain §3.2 (Vulnerability Signal) replaces battle-occurrence with Accord ≤ 1 territory-count thresholds (0-1 → +0; 2-3 → +1; 4-5 → +2; 6+ → +3). peninsular_strain §4.1 replaces battle-season +1 with per-territory +1 capped at +3/season. peninsular_strain §4.2 decouples decay from peninsula-wide peace, adds Treaty-pair decay (−1/pair, cap −2/season). peninsular_strain §4.4 reframes Strain/IP from 'no cross-feeding' to 'shared signal, independent aggregation'. mass_battle §E.2 retains immediate MS and Accord costs but redirects IP/Strain to deferred via peninsular_strain. mass_battle §E.4 caps reframed accordingly. Source: 2026-04-24 audit conversation P1-1 — prior mechanism inverted historical Pax Romana logic (Pax stabilizes after conquest; spec taxed conquest itself). Combination of fix-options (1)+(3) per audit conversation: held-instability advance + Treaty-pair decay.

ED-623 superseded by ED-743. ED-623's '+2 → +3 IP per battle-season' rate change addressed the symptom (insufficient threat manifestation in ST-42) but reinforced the inverted trigger; ED-743 relocates the trigger entirely. ST-42 was measuring battle-presence-as-signal under the old mechanism; the new mechanism's calibration requires fresh sim-validation. Note: this supersession invalidates ST-42's specific calibration finding while preserving the underlying observation that the prior +2 rate produced rarely-manifested threats — under the new mechanism, threat manifestation is governed by territorial-instability persistence rather than battle frequency.

Wound mechanic drift correction. ED-694 (Vitality = Endurance × 10, Max Wounds eliminated, ED-202 floor preserved) introduced a linear simplification that did not match the WI × (Max Wounds + 1) total-damage-capacity structure for Endurance values 1, 2, 3, 5, 7 (only End=4 and End=6 happened to align numerically). Concurrent canon variously specified wound penalties as −1D Pool (Combat) vs +1 Ob (Thread, mass Command, BG CF tactic) creating channel inconsistency. PP-716 reverts ED-694 simplification: stat name Health, formula = (Endurance + 6) × (Max Wounds + 1), MW = floor(Endurance/2) + 1, WI = Endurance + 6. Universal wound penalty: −1D Pool, no Ob, ever. designs/scene/derived_stats_v30.md §4.1 made authoritative source. combat_v30, threadwork_v30, params/combat, params/mass_combat all reference §4.1 rather than restating. Cross-references: PP-716, ED-694 (superseded), PP-232.
source: Jordan canonical clarification 2026-05-09; contradictions surfaced during combat_arch_residual_stress_01 R1 module work
jordan_decision: 2026-05-09 — wounds = −1D Pool universally, never Ob; Health = (End+6)×(MW+1); MW = floor(End/2)+1; revert ED-694; derived_stats §4.1 authoritative; propagate everywhere.

description: [RESOLVED 2026-05-10] Founded faction starting stats — specified for Stage 4 emergence (settlement_layer §6.2). Resolution: emerged faction at Declaration receives the following starting stats: L 2 (limited recognition; advances via diplomacy + Conviction fulfillment), PS 3 (grass-roots base, anchored by founding NPC officers), I (Influence) = floor(Renown/2) at Declaration (carries Declaration roll pool forward), W = 2 + (# settlements controlled at Declaration − 1) capped at 5 (founding economic base + per-settlement scaling), Mil 1 (militia only at founding), Int 2 (informal network), Sta 3 (loyal officers, stable orgs). Faction stats advance from these baselines via standard Domain Actions and recovery mechanics (Mandate Recovery, etc.). Affected docs: settlement_layer_v30.md §6.2 — add Starting Stats subsection.

description: [RESOLVED 2026-05-10] Treaty type qualification for §6.1 "Treaty-bound" effective hegemony. Resolution: Crown Treaty (victory_v30 §3.1), Capitulation (faction_layer §3.1), and Tributary (faction_layer §3.1) qualify as Treaty-bound for §6.1 effective hegemony purposes. Alliance, Peace, Commercial, and Truce do NOT qualify (parity relationships, not subordination). Rationale: §6.1 hegemony is about effective sovereignty over a territory's controlling faction — only subordination-treaties create that relationship. Affected docs: peninsular_strain_v30 §6.1 — add "Treaty-bound qualifying types" clarification; faction_layer_v30 §3.1 — add cross-reference to §6.1.

description: [RESOLVED 2026-05-10] Generic Peace treaty territory cession — resulting Accord. Resolution: voluntary cession in ratified Peace treaty (Phase 3 Success or Overwhelming, mutual benefit clause): Accord set to 2 (legitimacy inherited from negotiated handover). Forced cession in Capitulation (dictated submission): Accord set to 1 (resentment of imposed terms, like military conquest). Tributary arrangement: territory remains under prior controller's direct rule (no Accord change at agreement; Tributary affects Wealth flow + §6.1 hegemony qualification only). Affected docs: peninsular_strain_v30 §2.3 — add Peace treaty cession row; faction_layer_v30 §3.1 — add Accord effects column to Treaty Types.

description: [RESOLVED 2026-05-10] Renown grant for Conviction resolution states. Resolution: Fulfilled +1 Renown (existing canonical), Transformed +1 Renown (transformation through play counts — Conviction evolved meaningfully), Failed +0 Renown (the attempt grants Momentum +1 per §2.3, not Renown — Failed is mechanically distinct from Fulfilled), Unresolved +0 Renown (no engagement). Rationale: Renown rewards public deeds and accomplished arcs, not internal struggle alone. Failed Convictions get Momentum (engaging the attempt) but not Renown (no public accomplishment). Transformed Convictions reflect substantive character evolution — public-facing enough for Renown. Affected docs: player_agency_v30 §5.4 Renown Sources table — expand "Conviction fulfilled" row to "Conviction resolved (Fulfilled or Transformed)".
source: Sim audit 2026-05-10 (campaign walkthroughs v3, finding E10)

closure_commit: propagation: player_agency §5.4 - Renown table: Fulfilled OR Transformed grants +1

description: [RESOLVED 2026-05-10] Govern action on freshly-acquired territory — Prosperity inheritance. Resolution: Prosperity inherits unchanged from prior controller at moment of conquest/transfer. Order in affected settlements drops −2 at conquest moment (already canonical per peninsular_strain §2.4b "battle at settlement −2 immediately"). Govern Ob computed from inherited Prosperity, +1 Ob from Accord 1 penalty (canonical §2). The Order penalty cascades upward to Accord recovery — restoring Order via Govern Success restores Accord progression. No additional Prosperity penalty needed; Order is the mechanical pressure point. Affected docs: peninsular_strain_v30 §2.4 — add clarifying note "Prosperity inherits; Order penalty per §2.4b governs recovery dynamics".

description: Volley Power-only pool ignores Size — massing ranged units provides zero additional firepower (Size 2 XBow rolls same 3D as Size 5 XBow). Inconsistent with Engagement pool where min(Size,Command) contributes dice. Removes strategic depth in ranged composition. Source: A2 sim F-006.
jordan_decision: 2026-05-11 — Yes. Size affects Volley. Pool = min(Size,Power)+Power dice TN6. Replaces Power-only.

description: Orderly withdrawal mechanics missing — mass_battle §A.12 covers Rout/Pursuit but no rule for orderly Withdraw battle plan vs Advance pursuers. Speed differential (Standard vs Slow) should permit disengagement but no canonical procedure. Source: A2 sim F-007.
jordan_decision: 2026-05-11 — Yes. Withdraw plan + Speed advantage (faster than pursuer) = orderly disengage, no engagement.

description: Rally action mechanical specifics unclear — mass_battle §A.7 Phase 6 step 4 lists Rally as general action but no canonical formula (pool, Ob, effect on Morale vs Discipline, success/failure consequences). Source: A2 sim F-008.

description: Strain vs Turmoil terminology inconsistency — phases.md §4d uses Strain; tracks.md uses Turmoil; peninsular_strain uses both. Confirm whether these are the same track, sub-components of same system, or distinct mechanics. Source: A2 sim F-010.

description: Field fortification mechanics missing — mass_battle §A.9 covers terrain modifiers but no rule for player-constructed fieldworks (earthworks, trenches, barricades). Historical precedent is decisive. Needed for siege approach scenarios. Source: A2 sim F-011.

description: Combined multi-unit attack resolution unspecified — mass_battle §A.7 allows max 3 engagements but does not specify how 2+ units attacking one defender resolves. Separate rolls? Combined pool? Defender dice allocation across attackers? Source: A2 sim F-012.

description: Gransol (T8) Fort level not specified in core.md territory table — T3/T14 are Fort 3, T10 is Fort 2, but T8 Hafenmark capital has no Fort entry. Lake geography provides natural defense but needs formalization. Source: A2 sim F-015.

description: Lake-city siege mechanics unresolved — Gransol (landlocked lake, Switzerland-analog) cannot be fully encircled. Does lake access prevent starvation siege? Can Crown blockade an inland lake? Distinct from naval Port-to-Port mechanics. Source: A2 sim F-016.

description: Battle loss Stability trigger ambiguity — mass_battle Part E §E.1 says losing side Stab -1 immediate + §E.2 Stab -1 at Accounting = -2 per battle loss. Combined with territory loss trigger (-1), a single lost battle costs -3 Stability. At starting Stab 4, faction collapses in 2 battles. Verify this severity is intended — may need caps or graduated loss. Source: A2 sim F-017.

description: core.md Faction Starting Stats table has Crown Military 4 and Lowenritter (Split) Military 6. Per Jordan 2026-05-11: Crown Military is 5, Lowenritter Military is 5. Additionally Lowenritter is not a separate faction at game start — it is Crown military arm. Lowenritter stats only apply IF Split occurs (not guaranteed). core.md table needs update.

description: Lowenritter faction mechanics clarification per Jordan 2026-05-11. (1) Split outcome: Crown Military drops from 5 to 2 AND loses most units. (2) Coup outcome: Crown faction SUSPENDED until Lowenritter installs new monarch. If Torben/Elske/Lenneth = Crown continues. If Duke (Vaynard/Baralta) = Crown absorbed into that faction. (3) Lowenritter is NOT guaranteed to split or coup — only fires if they lose faith in Crown. core.md Graduated Autonomy table needs these outcomes formalized.
jordan_decision: 2026-05-11 — Split = Crown Mil 5->2 + unit loss. Coup = Crown suspended, monarch installed, faction follows monarch identity.

description: Engagement damage formula ambiguous. Phase 5 pool split does not specify whether damage = (net_successes * 1+Power) for attacker only, or (margin between off/def pools * 1+Power), or other. SIM-MB-04 INTERPRETATION-01 used margin model and produced one-turn kills via margins of 5-6. Needs explicit formula statement. Source: SIM-MB-04 FINDING-1.

description: Volley post-ED-800 double-amplified. ED-800 approved min(Size,Power)+Power dice. SIM-MB-04 shows Power 3 unit goes 3 to 6 dice while multiplier 1+Power remains, producing average 21.6 dmg/turn vs units with 9-16 HP. Either pool or multiplier needs recalibration. Source: SIM-MB-04 FINDING-2.
jordan_decision: 2026-05-11 — Volley damage = max(0, net_successes) - ranged_DR. No (1+Power) multiplier. Composition (ED-814) handles scaling via back_pct × volley pool. Attrition contributor, not decisive.

description: ED-801 withdrawal phase gate unspecified. Jordan ruling confirmed Withdraw plan + speed advantage = orderly disengage. SIM-MB-04 implemented as Phase 5 gate but it may be a Phase 3 gate instead, changing positioning logic materially. Source: SIM-MB-04 FINDING-7.

description: Discipline redefined as formation coherence under fire — capacity to maintain designed shape, hold assigned column position, execute standing orders while taking casualties. Replaces vague 'organizational integrity' framing. Penalty table interpretation: pool penalty represents shape drift, not generic disorder. A high-Discipline unit holds the horseshoe even as the center bleeds; a low-Discipline unit reverts to local survival behavior — fighting whoever is in front, abandoning flank positions, ignoring orders. Aligns with historical doctrine (Vegetius De Re Militari, Maurice Strategikon, Leo VI Taktika). Triangulates with Command (tactical authority — what tactics are available) and Power (raw combat capability — how hard the unit hits) for clean three-stat unit quality model. Existing formulas (H per Size segment, penalty tiers 5-7/3-4/1-2, Reform Phase Discipline restoration) all remain valid under new framing.
jordan_decision: 2026-05-11 — Discipline = formation coherence under fire. Sharpens Power/Discipline/Command triangle.

description: Unit shape mechanical signatures. Five canonical shapes with empirically validated values from SIM-MB-05A/B/C (6000+ trials). Line (Disc 1, Cmd 1, no modifiers, baseline; drifts to mob). Arrowhead (Disc 4, Cmd 2, center column +2D Off, flanks -1D Off; tactical concept wedge breakthrough; hard counter GappedLine). Horseshoe (Disc 5, Cmd 3, center -2D Off +1D Def, flanks +2D Off whenever ANY engagement is resolved at the Horseshoe center column — positional H-2 trigger from SIM-MB-05C, 54 percent mean winrate; tactical concept Cannae envelopment per Hannibal 216 BC). GappedLine (Disc 5, Cmd 3, gap column empty cannot engage, non-gap columns +2D Off when opponent concentrates more than 40 percent front-row mass in gap column; tactical concept kill-zone channel per Roman precedent). RefusedFlank (Disc 3, Cmd 2, refused column -2D Off, engaged columns +1D Off compensating mass; tactical concept Epaminondas oblique order at Leuctra 371 BC). All shapes drift to Line on Discipline failure per ED-817. Player selects shape at battle setup per ED-820. Composition (ED-814) auto-distributes troops across the 9-cell grid according to shape template. Replaces existing Formation Types section: Wedge becomes Arrowhead preset; Shield Wall becomes Line+heavy front composition; Skirmish becomes spread-Line; Feigned Retreat becomes proto-Horseshoe.
jordan_decision: 2026-05-11 — Five shapes ratified per SIM-MB-05A/B/C empirical validation. Horseshoe trigger resolved as positional H-2 (any engagement at center fires flank trap), historically matching Cannae precedent. Horseshoe flank trap magnitude +2D Off per calibration sweep. GappedLine and RefusedFlank conditional/compensating mechanics validated.

description: Shape failure cascade — direct drift to Line model, validated by SIM-MB-05A/B/C. When unit Discipline drops below shape minimum, shape immediately becomes Line at Phase 6 Step 2 (after Discipline degradation check). No intermediate stages, no linear chain. Grid re-distributed using Line template. Drift is irreversible within battle. Players cannot voluntarily switch back to original shape mid-battle (ED-820 emergency reshape Command Ob 3 is the only mid-battle shape change mechanism). Tiered drift alternative tested in SIM-MB-05C and produces 5-15 percentage points better outcomes for sub-min Discipline units but adds significant design complexity (5 drift chains to specify, more state to track) without dramatic gameplay improvement; direct-to-Line preserves design clarity that Discipline gates shape selection. If preferring tiered drift later, ED-817 may be revised — but empirical case for direct is cleaner.
jordan_decision: 2026-05-11 — Direct drift to Line, irreversible mid-battle. SIM-MB-05C tiered alternative empirically tested and rejected for simplicity reasons.

description: Horseshoe trigger mechanism — RESOLVED via SIM-MB-05C branch exploration. Four trigger variants tested over 320 trials each: H-1 mass>40 percent (initial proposal) 37 percent mean winrate; H-2 positional always (fires when any engagement at center) 54 percent mean winrate; H-3 pool-ratio>40 percent 41 percent; H-4 weak-column AI heuristic 54 percent. H-2 selected as canonical: simplest mechanism, achieves balanced winrate target (45-60 percent across all opponent shapes), historically accurate (at Cannae Hannibal did not require Roman wedge formation, only Roman commitment to attacking his center). Horseshoe flank trap fires whenever any opposing unit's engagement is resolved at the Horseshoe's center column, regardless of opponent composition or shape. Closed into ED-816 specification.
jordan_decision: 2026-05-11 — H-2 positional trigger ratified. Trap fires on engagement at center, not opponent composition. Folded into ED-816 final spec.

description: Volley/Engagement composition balance. SIM-MB-05A composition sweep showed ranged-heavy compositions winning disproportionately (pure-melee vs ranged-heavy 4 to 1 disadvantage). SIM-MB-05C tested TN6 vs TN7 Volley calibration: TN7 improves pure-melee-vs-balanced from 28 percent to 36 percent (8 percentage point gain) but pure-melee-vs-ranged-heavy remains roughly unchanged (15-16 percent). TN7 is partial improvement but does not fully resolve. Recommendation: adopt TN7 as clean primary fix; raise composition-balance secondary measure as ED-825 for Phase 3+ work. Secondary measure options: a) melee_pct as engagement Ob bonus for heavier-melee side; b) Volley pool cap = floor(volley_pool / 2); c) ranged_DR scaled by defender melee_pct (heavier front rank shields back rank). Source: SIM-MB-05C section 3.2.
jordan_decision: pending
stale_reason: Superseded — Recommended TN7 NOT adopted — live VOLLEY_TN=6 + density-scaled volley + Lanchester; SIM-MB-05 concern superseded by re-architecture.

description: Combined attack effectiveness calibration. SIM-MB-05C 3v1 combined-attack test surfaced concern: Fibonacci-divided pool concentrated at one column may be LESS effective than 3 separate column engagements. Lead Power 4 Size 3 Arrowhead with 2 supporters concentrating at center: combined pool ~7 dice vs defender 1 column ~3 dice. Compared to distributed: 3 attackers engaging 3 columns each at ~3 dice vs defender split ~3 dice each. Distributed produces 3 simultaneous Successful engagements; combined produces 1 Successful engagement against full defender HP pool. ED-805 Fibonacci denominators (1,2,3,5,8) may be too aggressive. Resolution options: a) reduce denominators (1, 1.5, 2, 3, 5); b) combined attack damages all defender columns proportionally; c) combined attack with single column requires defender to have a single-column shape (e.g., Arrowhead) to be tactically meaningful. Test harness for 3v1 combined was incomplete in SIM-MB-05C; needs SIM-MB-06 rebuild. Severity P2, defer until after Phase 6 propagation.
jordan_decision: pending
stale_reason: Superseded — Live engine per-cell support-stacking + puncture, not Fibonacci denominators; 'needs SIM-MB-06' overtaken by tests/sim/mass_battle.

description: F12 (DISCOVERED during ED-826/827 execution sweep, not in original engine NERS audit): params/combat L138 carries `Max Wounds = floor(Endurance/2) + 1` WITHOUT the PP-717 D1 cap at 3. The MW cap is canonical in derived_stats §4.1 L57 but was never propagated to params/combat. At End 6: params says MW=4 (Health = 50 × 5 = 250 HP, wildly wrong); canon says MW=3 (Health = 48). description: F13 (DISCOVERED during Phase 5 sim 2026-05-15 verification): params/core.md per-die EV table and probability quick-reference table both computed using face 10 = +1 success, NOT the canonical face 10 = +2 successes (per valoria_master_document §1.1, atom 03__1-1-die-face-rule-d10). Canonical values: TN 7 → E[net]/die = 0.40 (not 0.30); 10D pool → P(≥1) = 92% (not 99%). Engine code reading these tables computes wrong dice probabilities by 33%. Ratchet-shifts all downstream balance analyses (build matrices, Ob calibration) that referenced these tables. description: Decision E 2026-05-15: continuous engine adopted as canonical specification for videogame (Godot) implementation. The engine is now specified two ways with statistically equivalent outputs: discrete (d10 dice, legacy/TTRPG mode) and continuous (Normal-distribution sampling, videogame mode). Continuous enables fractional Ob, fractional TN (already canonical for combat per PP-717 Fiore), and continuous degree resolution (outcome magnitude = net − Ob). Phase 5 sim 2026-05-15 validated equivalence (max deviation 0.029 in mean, 0.022 in std at pool sizes 5-17). Derived stat formulas, attribute scale, Ob scale, degree thresholds all unchanged. params/core.md §Continuous Engine is the canonical spec. derived_stats §1 acknowledges engine duality.
jordan_decision: 2026-05-15 — adopt continuous engine; canonical for videogame implementation.

description: Phase 7 sim 2026-05-15 (tests/sim/phase7_action_triangle_2026-05-15.md) demonstrated empirically that the Strike/Feint/Full Guard action triangle is a load-bearing combat balance mechanism. In Strike-only play, Fast (Agi 6, pool 17D) wins 96.7% conditional vs Strong (Agi 3, pool 11D); when Strong actively employs PP-294 Feint, Fast wins drop to 34.9% (Strong wins 65% of decisive duels). End-dominance (Tough vs Strong) similarly collapses from 82% Strike-only to ~7% with tactical play. Phase 4-7 chain conclusion: pool formula is NOT the structural driver of combat dominance; action triangle is. Decision A (revert PP-717 D2) vindicated; pool softcap was patching the wrong layer. Doubling decision becomes design-feel, not balance, choice. Documented in params/combat.md as Combat Balance Note section. Phase 8 with better-tuned Smart AI pending to confirm magnitude.
jordan_decision: 2026-05-15 — document Phase 4-7 finding as canonical balance note.

description: WS-H-4 resolution (workstream meta-audit 2026-05-15). Phase 7 End-dominance "Tough 82% Strike-only → ~7% with tactical play" result is likely a sim artifact: same-pool matchups (both 11D) with same Smart AI rules should produce similar outcomes between Strike-only and Smart modes, but the numbers shift dramatically. Most likely cause: Full Guard / Take Breath interaction in Smart mode that breaks the symmetric expectation. Combat Balance Note in params/combat.md previously canonized both the direction AND the magnitude; this edit retains the direction (tactical sensitivity exists) but defers the magnitude pending Phase 8 confirmation with better-tuned Smart AI. Agi-dominance findings (96.7% → 34.9% via underdog Feinting) are unaffected — they rest on the asymmetric Underdog Feints mode which is more trustworthy than Smart-vs-Smart.
jordan_decision: 2026-05-15 — execute REC-1 from post-audit recommendations.

description: WS-D-1 resolution (workstream meta-audit 2026-05-15). Decision E (continuous engine, ED-833) canonized the engine for all systems but Phase 5 sim validated only at combat parameters (pool 5-17D, TN 7). This is a documentation gap: the validation IS system-agnostic by Central Limit Theorem (Normal-approximation of d10 pool sums holds for any system using d10 dice), so the canonical scope is mathematically defensible. Edit adds explicit "Validation scope" paragraph to params/core.md §Continuous Engine acknowledging this — combat-validated; system-agnostic by CLT; faction-scale at small-pool boundary warrants dedicated sanity-check sim. No canonical change; clarification only.
jordan_decision: 2026-05-15 — execute REC-2 from post-audit recommendations.

description: Phase 8 sim (tests/sim/phase8_smart_ai_v2_2026-05-15.md) refines Phase 7 finding (ED-834): the "34.9% Feint inverts dominance" claim was partly stamina-management artifact. Phase 7 Underdog mode had Fast=strike_only (no Take Breath); Fast exhausted around round 5, losing many duels by stam exhaustion rather than damage. When both sides manage stamina via Smart v2 (Take Breath threshold ≤8 + PP-294-aware Feint alternation), Fast vs Strong reverts to ~100% Fast cond win; Tough vs Strong settles at 63.7% (real moderate End-dominance, not Phase 7 7.5% artifact). Calibration: symmetric Agi 3 vs Agi 3 produces 51.2% — sim balanced. Revised reading: pool advantage at canonical attribute differences is structurally significant in skilled play; the action triangle modifies but does not bridge a 6-die pool gap. Decision A (revert PP-717 D2) reopened pool dominance NOT compensated by action triangle alone. Combat Balance Note in params/combat.md updated with Phase 8 subsection. Whether to act on this (re-ratify PP-717 D2, drop doubling, pool cap, accept design) is a Jordan decision deferred.
jordan_decision: 2026-05-15 — proceed working; update Balance Note with Phase 8 refinement

description: Phase 10 sim (tests/sim/phase10_str_stam_reform_2026-05-16.md) tested two design-question reforms (canonical stamina formula, STR bonus dice on offense) and discovered a sim-side correction with retroactive implications. Primary finding (correction, not reform): Phase 4 through Phase 9 sims used Stamina = 15 + End * 2 (range 21-29) but canonical formula per ED-694 is Stamina = End * 5 (range 15-35) with Take Breath restoring partial stamina (End * 2 not full pool) and +1 stam cost per wound. Prior sims systematically underweighted Endurance. With canonical stamina, every End-investing matchup shifts 4-9pp from prior sim baseline; Fast vs Titan moves from 81.8% to 72.6% before any reform. Phase 8 finding ED-838 (Tough vs Strong 63.7% End-dominance) understates magnitude — direction unchanged, magnitude larger. Secondary finding (reform proposal, pending ratification): STR bonus dice floor(STR/3) on Strike and Disarm offense pool provides 2-6pp uplift to high-STR compensator builds. Best config under canonical stam + STR-strong: Fast vs Titan 67.5/32.5 — meaningful upset rate. Persistent finding: pool dominance survives all reforms. Fast (Agi 6) vs Titan (Agi 3 + max comp) finishes 70/30 even with undouble + 1/End Ob + Disarm + canonical stam + STR-strong; further levers (pool cap past Agi 4, static defense, smaller pools) untested. STR + light blade gap: Mighty (Agi 3, STR 7, light) sits at 98.5% loss to Fast — light weapon str_mult 1.0 + 2pp hit-rate gain from bonus dice cannot overcome 3-die pool gap. Either accept (STR builds wield heavy) or design specific light-weapon STR mechanics. Methodology corrections also recorded: TN vs Ob conflation in prior wound-penalty sims (Ob is net-success threshold not per-die TN shift); Disarm AI threshold bug fixed (opp_weapon_mod >= 3 not >= 4). Combat Balance Note in params/combat.md updated with Phase 10 subsection.
jordan_decision: 2026-05-16 — commit and proceed

description: Royal Progress Ob formula contradicts its own descriptive text. Part 10 §3.2 (Mode I — Royal Progress) canonical statement is: "Ob | floor(sum of own territories Accord / 2) — easier when Accord is high (people aren't restive); harder when realm is in turmoil". The formula floor(sum_accord/2) produces the OPPOSITE behavior from the descriptive clause: as sum_accord rises, Ob rises (action gets HARDER), not easier. Surfaced by M7 balance sweep N=1000 (commit d94f1e3e): Crown Royal Progress fails at game-start because starting sum_accord ≈ 30-33 across 6 Crown territories produces Ob 15-16 vs Pool = Influence (5) + Standing (0) = 5; mathematically unwinnable without modifier stacking. Possible resolutions: (a) revise formula to floor((max_possible - sum) / 2) where max_possible = 7 × num_owned_territories, making low-Accord realms HARDER not easier; (b) revise descriptive text to say "harder when Accord is high (king must show face to many contented subjects across long itineraries)" — but this contradicts the Henry I / Elizabeth I historical anchor; (c) accept the formula as-is, treat Royal Progress as a recovery-from-crisis action (low Accord → low Ob) and revise descriptive text accordingly. M6 implements as canon literally says (option c interpretation by default). Source: M7 balance sweep + Pass-3 finding #4.
jordan_decision: Option A ratified 2026-05-17 (Jordan directive earlier this session for iteration; formula matches descriptive text)
resolution:
Ob formula iterated to max(2, floor((sum_max - sum_current) / 2)) where
sum_max = 7 * num_owned_territories, sum_current = sum of own-territory
Accord values. Matches descriptive text ("easier when Accord is high").
Floor 2 prevents trivial auto-success at peak Accord. Cost (W -2),
Pool (I + Standing modifier), outcome table unchanged. part10 §3.2
amended with iteration note + verification table at illustrative Crown
states. Design intent clarified: Royal Progress is maintenance action,
not crisis recovery (use Coronation Renewal or Great Work for crisis).

description: Faction collapse cascade loops. description: sigma-leverage engine Ob-floor (P-232) conflict: the engine applied advantage as an Ob reduction (eff_ob = base_Ob - eff_sigma*sigma_N), driving Effective Ob below 1 on ordinary favourable stacks -- violating canon P-232 (Obstacle Scale: Ob minimum 1; no modifier may reduce Ob below 1) -- and making the Overwhelming threshold (2*Eff_Ob) nonsensical when Ob went negative. Exposed F3 too: hardcoded SIGMA_N_COEFF=0.8 (TN7 sigma) miscalibrated impact magnitude off-TN7.
resolution: Reformulated advantage as a mu-shift (net_boost boosts the expected net: mean += eff_sigma*sigma*sqrt(N)); base_Ob and TN unchanged, so P-232 is never triggered and the degree ladder stays intact. Identical odds at TN7, TN-exact at TN6/8 (closes F3). eff_ob retained as an Ob-floored display helper only.
alternative_rejected: Exempting the sigma-transform from P-232 (a canon change) -- rejected: leaves F3 and the degree-ladder bug unsolved.

description: Faction Domain Action resolver — RATIFIED (Jordan 2026-05-31, 'works well'). At faction/strategic scale, bare-stat dice pools (1-7D) are statistically degenerate: a Military-2 faction succeeds at a Domain Action at only P~=0.070 @ Ob 3 (ER-1 / diagnostic F1; authoritative die rule, verified mu). Resolution: aggregate / deterministic+stochastic (sum relevant unit/derived values; CK3 / EU / Stellaris / KoDP / Six Ages precedent for acclaimed faction-scale resolution). ER-1/ER-2 + precedent INDEPENDENTLY justify the change (engine_replacement_audit L271); it does NOT rest on GD-2 (see ED-865 - GD-2 governs action-SELECTION only). SCOPE: faction/strategic layer only - the continuous graded resolver is KEPT at personal/provincial scale (repair-and-keep dominates at core, audit L290). Open prerequisite: F2 Ob-formula contradiction (ED-882-zone).

[GATE G8 - Jordan decision] Mass-battle low-Command sigma-leverage. Command sigma-leverage is HIGH at low Command (armature A6, computed): ~0.500/pt at Cmd 2, decaying to in-band at Cmd 4-7 - low-Command early-game generals get oversized per-point leverage (same small-pool sigma non-uniformity as ED-865/874, mass-battle instance). Closes attribute_weight_standard GAP #2. Candidate fix: apply the d+s resolver to mass-battle Command (per Stage-4, fixable only by deterministic+stochastic). PENDING Jordan: adopt the d+s fix, or accept low-Command leverage as intended early-game texture. | RESOLVED 2026-06-20 (Jordan resolve-all; mechanical-tier, vetoable): keep the high low-Command sigma-leverage as intended. Bottom-up [READ: orchestration.py base_combat_pool COMMAND_SIGMA]: pool=Command*(1+cohesion), so +1 Command is relatively larger at low Command (2->3=+33% base vs 6->7=+14%) -- command quality matters most where scarce. Top-down anchor: generalship historically had outsized effect on small/poorly-led forces (rout cascades under poor command); acclaimed strategy designs (Total War, Field of Glory) make leadership leverage high for small/elite units. COMMAND_SIGMA model stays as-is; magnitude defensible. Precision retune, if wanted, needs a sigma-sweep (out of scope).
source: faction diagnostic consolidated candidates; armature A6; faction handoff GATE G8

description: General death as a consequence of mass battle eliminated — capture/incapacitation is the worst battlefield outcome for any named character; no mass-battle mechanic kills a player or named character.
decision: Reframed three sites in mass_battle_v30.md from death to incapacitation/capture: A.5 'General two-stage death'->'two-stage incapacitation' (Stage 2 killed->incapacitated/captured-if-field-lost; mechanical effect -2 morale/Command=0/uncommanded unchanged; general survives); A.4 morale triggers (General killed->incapacitated/captured; -2 + -5 max unchanged); D.2 officer death->incapacitation/capture (1d20<=Size-lost roll outcome reframed; companion 'combat death'->battlefield capture). Added a canonical 'No character death from mass battle' clause. The engine emits an 'incapacitated/captured' state + which side holds the field; the post-capture consequence (ransom/imprisonment/escape/return) is a world-layer event rendered OUTSIDE the mass-battle container (not designed here). Mechanical magnitudes/timing unchanged — fictional outcome only.

description: Canonical Size-based combat pool (min(Size,Command)+Command; derived_stats L276 / PP-233) set aside for the mass-battle engine resolution per Jordan canon-structure directive 2026-06-02. It made per-capita effectiveness size-dependent (degrades below Command), contaminating the Lanchester melee LINEAR law to an emergent exponent ~1.7 instead of 1. Command additionally redefined as a DERIVED stat.
decision: Mass-battle base exchange pool driven SOLELY by Command (sigma-leverage quality): pool = COMMAND_POOL_MULT*Command (=2*Command, the canonical value at Size>=Command); numbers (Size) enter outcomes ONLY via the Lanchester frontage term. Command DERIVED = clamp(round((2*Charisma + Cognition)/3), 1, 7) -- Charisma primary weight, Cognition secondary. Validated by conserved-quantity exponent (no-rout primitive): melee restored to true Lanchester LINEAR (p~1.0, conserves A-B), volley stays SQUARE (p~2.0), coefficient-invariant (K sets timescale not law); magnitude historical (winner-low/loser-high per du Picq victors <5%, no-annihilation rout-decided, square concentration 17.8x Cannae/Pharsalus). Gated COMMAND_SIGMA_ENABLED (default ON; OFF byte-exact to the prior canonical pool, verified isolated). Committed 2cf3feb6. Constants COMMAND_POOL_MULT=2 / CMD_CHA_WEIGHT=2 / CMD_COG_WEIGHT=1 class-B sim-tunable, Jordan-confirmed. FOLLOW-UP (not yet applied): canon DOC reconciliation -- params/mass_combat.md PP-233 pool + derived_stats Command derivation; engine leads, docs to follow.

description: RATIFY + CANONIZE all 2026-06-05 mass-battle session work per Jordan ('ratify, canonize, commit all'). Engine-side (tests/sim/mass_battle/orchestration.py). (a) DISCIPLINE DECOUPLED FROM MOVEMENT SPEED (afdabadf): the disc_mult-on-actual_speed/step coupling was engine-introduced with no canonical basis; PP-232 renames Cohesion->Discipline and discipline IS cohesion (Power penalty A.4, degradation under Size-loss asymmetry, Reform restoration, H formula); params/mass_combat.md has ZERO discipline x movement coupling. Coupling removed (actual_speed = base + stance_mod); disc_mult retained only where canonical (node cohesion factor k, wheel rate kw). (b) reform_check WIRED (8ec085c0) + CANON-CORRECTED (34db3c91): empty G-8 hook now restores +1 discipline toward discipline_start for a unit not in melee contact, gated by the full L180-183 rule -- general's Command >= current Discipline + 1 AND Command >= 2 (PP-241); Command=1 cannot restore; Command caps reformable discipline. (c) DIRECTED MANEUVER PATHING (8ec085c0): units no longer always path straight at nearest. envelop = go wide past the enemy's nearer flank then hook into its flank/rear; IMPLEMENTS the existing canonical Envelopment tactic (A.8) and extends the canonical wing-pathing precedent (A.3b L125 Horseshoe). surge = break a line and drive deep past the enemy front (penetration); gap-fill, no prior canonical tactic. Subunit.maneuver + maneuver_side, also fired by instructions tokens (Flanker envelop template now live); _maneuver_target stateless per-tick; advance_cells direct column-local+wheel path is byte-exact default. (d) TARGETING GRANULARITY CONFIRMED unit->subunit->cell. (e) PC_NODE_COHESION continuous-path KeyError FIXED (545af082) + self-healing fabrication gate / ledger auto-bootstrap (88479e58); infra.
decision: All canonical. Discipline = cohesion (PP-232), NOT a movement-speed input -- disc_mult-on-speed permanently struck. reform_check is the canonical L180-183 reform with the PP-241 command gate. envelop is the engine implementation of the A.8 Envelopment tactic; surge is a canonical class-B breakthrough/penetration maneuver (gap-fill). Maneuver constants MANEUVER_SURGE_DEPTH=5, MANEUVER_ENVELOP_WIDE=4, REFORM_ENGAGE_DIST=1 are class-B sim-tunable (ratified, Jordan-vetoable). Targeting granularity unit->subunit->cell is the confirmed canonical structure.
top_down_anchors: envelop: Hannibal double envelopment at Cannae (216 BC); Total War directed flanking. surge: Theban deep column breaking the Spartan line at Leuctra (371 BC); Napoleonic ordre-mixte breakthrough column; operational-wargame breakthrough mechanics. reform: standard cohesion-recovery-when-disengaged in operational wargaming.
bottom_up_sources: engine assign_targets + advance_cells (column-local + PC_WHEEL overhang) traced; maneuvers extend the existing per-cell pather. canon: mass_battle_v30.md A.8 Envelopment, A.3b L125 wing pathing, L180-183 reform / PP-241, L7 PP-232 rename; params/mass_combat.md (zero speed coupling).
validation: counters deepColumn 5/8 ShieldWall 8/8 command 8/8 byte-identical across all changes; fuzz(120) 0 engfail/0 degen; reform cmd5/d3->4, cmd4/d4 no-change, cmd1/cmd2 closed; envelop hooks flank (lateral 7 > flank 5, row 17), surge breaks weak line (row 10 vs direct 16).

description: APPLY ratified de-saturation + venue bar-balancing pass to the ground-up contest engine (designs/audit/2026-06-03-contest-groundup/). (a) DE-SATURATION: engine.OVERWHELM_SIGMA=0.85; degree() gains an optional pool arg -> live Overwhelming bar = MU_PER_DIE*pool + 0.85*SD_PER_DIE*sqrt(pool), so the degree-3 rate stays bounded across pool sizes (pool-8 0.44->0.15) instead of saturating; pool-less degree(net,ob) legacy path UNCHANGED so direct-call unit tests keep meaning, only _reception takes the gated bar. (b) RES_FLOOR=0.15 in resolver: a hostile/off-axis reception can't zero an advance's resonance (raw resonance dips to 0.097 at secret_council/pathos, so the floor binds). (c) WIN-CONDITION BAR SWAPS in modes.py: court ProofBar 4.0->2.0, inquisition 3.0->2.5, excommunication 5.0->3.0, appeal Grace 7.0->5.0, memorial 6.0->5.5, imperial Grace 8.0->5.5. (d) BALLOTTA: secret_council ThresholdRace(3.0) -> VoteAtClose(jurors=5, sharpness=0.6, noise=0.8), un-fusing the verdict from momentum (ROUT 62%->38%, SPLIT_DECISION now reachable 0.11). (e) NEW counterpuncher policy: confirmatio-then-refutatio (build first half, rebut the opponent's lead in the back half when behind); first draft was a dominated pure-rebut policy (lost 26-374), redesigned to viable (0.60-0.74) and the strongest drama generator. (f) tests re-baselined (skill-gap decisive 0.6->0.25, overwhelming-succession 0.5->0.20; both fell because de-saturation moved mass to committee/split while preserving the skill tilt A~12:1 over B), secret_council assertion -> VoteAtClose, 5 stale cross-cultural labels corrected, +5 new regression checks. 151/151 unit tests green. HELD (NOT in this ratification): imperial sovereign discipline 0.05->0.25 -- handoff lists it in both the ratified-apply and Jordan-owned sets with a third value (0.20) in evidence; left at 0.05 pending Jordan; the imperial GraceThreshold swap was applied separately. FINDINGS for Jordan (not changed): drama floor missed by the five asymmetric bar/grace venues (intrinsic to threshold dynamics, not a tuning bug); imperial & memorial courtier-dominated 0.92/0.99 with logos-family at 0% (discipline lever does not fix -- venue ethos-weighting does); counterpuncher cannot see venue.allow_rebuttal (ContestView gap) so self-destructs in no-rebuttal venues; 7v1 succession ~73% split (faction-layer legibility, sec 7.2.1); narrative.py stale 'SPLIT only under VoteAtClose' comment. P8 venue-doc citation hygiene remains a follow-up.
decision: De-saturation (OVERWHELM_SIGMA, pool-aware degree-3), RES_FLOOR, the six bar swaps, the secret_council ballotta, and the counterpuncher policy are applied and verified (class-B sim-tunable, Jordan-vetoable). Imperial sovereign discipline stays 0.05 (held). The drama-floor shortfall on asymmetric venues and the imperial/memorial courtier monoculture are surfaced as Jordan design calls, NOT ratification blockers.
top_down_anchors: counterpuncher: Quintilian, Institutio Oratoria -- partes orationis, confirmatio before refutatio (prove your case, then rebut). ballotta: Venetian/Athenian secret-ballot courts severing the counted verdict from the room (dikasteria; thorubos vs the vote). de-saturation: contest-engine diagnostic Lesson 2; RES_FLOOR: Lesson 6.
bottom_up_sources: engine.degree/roll_net + MU_PER_DIE/SD_PER_DIE traced; resolver._reception/_advance resonance formula; resolver.VoteAtClose (jurors/sharpness/noise), REBUT_CAP, Reserve.COST['rebut']=3; modes.VENUES + CROSS_CULTURAL_VENUES; narrative.summarize shape taxonomy. Chronicle re-measured N=80/ordered-pair 6-policy round-robin 5v5 + asymmetric accuser sweeps N=600.
validation: 151/151 unit tests; chronicle: no shape >50% (max inquisition ROUT 0.44), COLLAPSE 0.67-1.0 with fault-prone policies, >=2 viable approaches in 8/10 venues; excommunication ethos-accuser 0.02->0.37 at bar 3.0; counterpuncher zero-fault/zero-exhaustion in rebuttal venues; de-saturation pool-8 degree-3 0.44->0.15; res-floor min raw resonance 0.097<0.15.

title: FM three-level command architecture (mass battle, videogame)
description: Football-Manager-style three-level compositional command architecture for mass battle (videogame realization), adopted across design conversations 2026-05-15 (FM as canonical command reference; mentality > team instructions > roles > player instructions; historical-general / Cannae model) and 2026-06-02 (allocation grid + team-instruction layer + intervention windows). LEVELS: (1) Army doctrine [Unit] - general sets doctrine plus a team-instruction layer: Aggression {Cautious, Balanced, Pressing} and Cohesion-priority {Hold shape, Adapt}, conditioning how subordinate formations behave under stress. (2) Unit formation [Subunit] - each company adopts a formation from a default template OR via the allocation grid, an adjustable allocation surface where the player sets intent (e.g. '60% melee, horseshoe, hold defensive') and the system auto-distributes, with manual per-cell override ('the FM analogy is exact'). (3) Subunit role/subformation - company-internal geometry plus assigned role. CADENCE: deploy formations -> set doctrine -> assign units to roles -> ~5-20 intervention windows per battle (set intent, watch, intervene at punctuated decision points; not per-tick control). NOTE: 'ED-814', the label by which this was informally cited, was never a real ledger entry; this entry replaces it. Provenance reconstructed from conversations; fidelity confirmation invited (Jordan instructed filing 2026-06-09). | RATIFIED 2026-06-20 (Jordan accept/ratify/resolve-all): fidelity confirmed -- Jordan's FM three-level restatement (army strategy/formation -> sub-unit troop-type+subformation+tactic -> each cell inherits best tactic-execution) matches this filing.
