title: Echelon mapping + formation/subformation distinction
description: Echelon mapping and formation/subformation distinction (Jordan ruling, 2026-06-09 session). ECHELON MAPPING (three echelons, not four): battalion = army = Unit (the general's whole force; carries doctrine and the allocation grid per ED-907); company = Subunit (carries the formation/subformation); squad = cell/troops (emergent). FORMATION vs SUBFORMATION: a subformation is company-internal geometry, realized as Subunit.shape (a single body's arrangement; bottom-up). PLACEMENT (per ED-909): the Unit-level strategic postures (Envelopment, Refused Flank) are NOT Subunit shapes; they are army-level and emerge from sub-unit positions plus assigned flanking roles, realized as allocation-grid deployment presets, not maintained shapes. Company-level (Subunit) declarable geometries are Line, Arrowhead (wedge), and GappedLine.

title: Formation-taxonomy reconciliation (echelon split + terminology); supersedes ED-816 in part
description: Reconciliation of the two formation taxonomies at the echelon level (Jordan adjudication 2026-06-09, resolving options a+b+c). (a) SUPERSEDE: ED-816 canonized five shapes (Line, Arrowhead, Horseshoe, GappedLine, RefusedFlank) as empirically-validated sub-unit shapes; Horseshoe and RefusedFlank are removed from the Subunit shape set. Engine re-architecture required (they leave Subunit.shape) - approved as part of a known-needed engine revamp. (b) SPLIT, historically validated: Unit-level (army deployments, multi-body, emergent) = Envelopment [double envelopment; precedent Cannae 216 BC, Hannibal - center yields concave while wings + cavalry wrap flank and rear] and Refused Flank [oblique/refused order; precedent Leuctra 371 BC, Epaminondas; Leuthen 1757, Frederick the Great]. Sub-unit-level (single-body geometries) = Line [basic fighting line], Arrowhead/Wedge [cuneus / breakthrough], GappedLine [open/gapped order; the ED-816 wedge-counter; cf. Roman manipular quincunx gaps]. (c) TERMINOLOGY: 'Horseshoe' and 'RefusedFlank' are retired as Subunit.shape names. 'Envelopment' (army-level emergent posture, subsuming colloquial 'horseshoe'/'double envelopment') and 'Refused Flank' (army-level deployment) are reserved for the Unit level - matching the existing mass_battle_v30 tactics of those names. A company whose cells curve concave is exhibiting emergent wing-pathing, not a declared shape. RESIDUAL (still to detail): the full mapping between the A.6 dice-modifier formations (Shield Wall, Wedge, Skirmish, Column, Feigned Retreat, Reserve) and the geometric shapes - working hypothesis is orthogonal axes (geometry = Subunit.shape x posture = A.6 dice stance), e.g. Shield Wall = a Line geometry in a defensive posture; Wedge = Arrowhead geometry in an offensive posture. Flagged for a follow-up decision. FOLLOW-UP: ED-816 to be amended and the engine re-architected (Horseshoe/RefusedFlank Subunit.shape -> Unit-level emergent presets) as part of the revamp.
citations: designs/provincial/mass_battle_v30.md A.6 FORMATION TYPES; Envelopment/Refused Flank tactics
historical precedent: Cannae 216 BC; Leuctra 371 BC; Leuthen 1757

title: Phase-dependence finding: FM26 IP/OOP does not map to melee (open)
description: Phase-dependence finding (open; Jordan decision pending). FM26's In-Possession/Out-of-Possession two-shape model does NOT map cleanly to Valoria mass-battle melee. Disanalogy: football possession is exclusive (a clean binary), whereas Valoria melee is simultaneous mutual offence/defence - per A.7 each sub-unit declares an Offence/Defence pool split in Phase 1 (any allocation, min 1D each, secret, revealed Phase 5) and at Phase 5 Engagement both sides roll offence AND defence at once; a unit is never purely 'in' or 'out of possession'. The offensive/defensive control FM's split would add already exists: the per-turn Off/Def pool split (a continuous dial) + the formation posture (Wedge offensive / Shield Wall defensive) + Prepared Defence, all re-declarable at the ~5-20 intervention windows. The only genuinely new element is within-moment situational switching (auto-adopting a defensive posture when charged/flanked) - and the surprise-charge gap that addresses is already punished by the per-cell flank/fixed-by-other mechanics and mitigated by declaring Shield Wall in anticipation. RECOMMENDATION: do NOT import the two-formation IP/OOP split; if playtesting shows a real surprise-charge pain point, add only a light reactive-defensive-posture (a 'form to receive' reaction built from existing Shield Wall / Prepared Defence primitives) - playtest-gated, not built now. Grounds: NERS-Elegant, the over-engineering guard, and the Total-War 'no plethora of toggles' lesson. | RESOLVED 2026-06-20 (follows ED-907 ratification; vetoable): FM IP/OOP two-shape possession model is NOT adopted for Valoria melee -- structural disanalogy stands (football possession exclusive/binary; melee simultaneous mutual offence/defence). The adopted FM analogy is the tactical-command hierarchy (ED-907), orthogonal to IP/OOP. Settled consistent with Jordan's ED-907 ratification.
citations: designs/provincial/mass_battle_v30.md A.7 (Off/Def pool split; Phase 5 simultaneous offence+defence)

title: P1 supersession-scope gap: ranged / group / thread-in-combat have no engine resolution (open — Jordan)
description: Comprehensive personal-combat audit 2026-06-09 (designs/audit/2026-06-09-personal-combat-comprehensive/comprehensive_analysis_personal_combat.md, F1). combat_engine_v1 is ratified as THE canonical personal-combat resolver superseding the RESOLUTION layer of combat_v30 (ED-900/ED-904), but the engine resolves strictly 1v1 melee. Ranged combat (combat_v30 §5: LP TN7 / HP TN6 / sling TN8, ranged DR table, PP-720 friendly fire), group combat >2 (§8: zone collapse, Fibonacci offence bonus PP-216, rescue, multi-engagement), and thread-in-combat (§10) exist ONLY in the superseded resolution layer with no engine counterpart. canonical_sources wording vs the 2026-06-02 ratification proposal diverge on whether these are retained or orphaned. DECISION (Jordan): (a) declare them RETAINED-canonical on the combat_v30 dice chassis pending engine extension — annotate canonical_sources + combat_v30 header to declare the two-resolver seam; or (b) scope an engine extension. Until adjudicated, personal combat is incomplete at system scope (NERS-R fail in the audit verdict).
OWN-5 defection cascade (L-DEFECT, B1.2/B1.3) resolution. Was UNBUILT (loop_safety_ledger FAIL NERS-R). Jordan delegated best-judgment 2026-06-09.
decision: GAP-FILL RESOLVED (vetoable; Jordan delegated 'best judgment' 2026-06-09). OWN-5 = L-DEFECT relational defection cascade (B1.2/B1.3), marked UNBUILT in loop_safety_ledger (FAIL NERS-R, section-7 hooks only). DECISION: build as specced with an explicit fragility-multiplier (loop gain) + suppression-brake (player damper), sized so the loop is BOTH damped and bounded (resolution-diagnostic Lesson 5). MECHANIC: (a) TRIGGER relational edge-break (loyalty severed by defeat/betrayal/faction-weakening); (b) FRAGILITY-MULTIPLIER each completed defection raises faction fragility, lowering remaining members' defection threshold (the positive-feedback gain); (c) SPILLOVER cascade reaches relational neighbours at 0.5 strength/hop (geometric 0.5^hop -> bounded reach) [DAMPER, spatial]; (d) STRAIN damper -1/s (reuse DC-8 strain, gain-verified mean-reverting); (e) SUPPRESSION-BRAKE holder's Suppress action = -1 to the defection check per suppression, bounded (mirrors ED-853 insurgency suppression) [DAMPER, player]; (f) CAP tier-3 (mass defection) rare by threshold; cascade bounded by finite membership + strain-tier ceiling. Net per-cycle gain <1 via (c)+(d)+(e); bounded via (f) -> passes Lesson 5. BOTTOM-UP: loop_safety_ledger L-DEFECT (KOEI model; strain -1/s, 1/2 spillover, tier-3 rare); npc_relational_graph_v30 section-7 hooks; ED-853 suppression sibling. TOP-DOWN: published design (KOEI RTK/Nobunaga loyalty-defection; Paradox CK vassal-defection cascades w/ liege suppression brake) + threshold-cascade theory (Granovetter threshold model; Kuran preference-falsification, 1989 Eastern Bloc - each visible defection lowers the next holdout's threshold = the fragility-multiplier). BUILD STAGED (not landed here): author into npc_relational_graph_v30 section-7 / political_hierarchy B1.2-B1.3 as a PP; magnitudes sim-tunable. Closes OWN-5 / loop-safety section-9 step 3 (decision); roadmap pending -> resolved.

decision: Built per ED-1000: tier-laddered cascade + hop-attenuated 1/2-per-hop spillover (section-6.2) + Fragility-multiplier (gain, cap +3, -1/s decay) + Suppression-brake (-1/check, ED-853 sibling) + tier-3 hard cap. Loop damped+bounded (resolution-diagnostic Lesson 5). canonical_sources SHA refreshed (co-file). approved_by: Jordan (directive 2026-06-15 'smooth pools / fix discipline'; path 1 of documented fork selected); implemented by Claude
title: Smooth cohesion combat pool + continuous discipline penalty -- fixes command-sigma mirror side-bias
description: ED-899's flat command-sigma pool (COMMAND_POOL_MULT*Command, size-decoupled) produced a reproducible side-B win bias in mirror matchups (H1 Line-Line ~62/38 among decisive, |A-B|=21.7pp at n=60, p~0.003; also contaminated cavalry C1/C3 with spurious B wins). Root cause (full bottom-up diagnosis 2026-06-15): the command-sigma EXCHANGE math is provably side-symmetric (pools, net successes, sigma/angle, first-turn Lanchester contacting strength all symmetric to <1 SE); the flat 2*Command pool had NO smooth own-casualty degradation, leaving the discipline-penalty STEP as the SOLE pool-degradation term, which amplified a tiny unpinnable latent contact-geometry asymmetry (~0.9% first-turn frontage) into the mirror bias. Fix (Jordan path 1): (1) SMOOTH POOL -- command-sigma base is now Command*(1 + hp/hp_max): 2*Command at full strength (size-decoupled, ED-899 preserved; cohesion is a FRACTION so per-capita effectiveness stays size-independent, Lanchester exponent ~1), degrading smoothly to Command at annihilation; dilutes the discipline term so it can no longer dominate -> mirror side-symmetric (n=120: A=53%/B=47% decisive, |A-B|=3.3pp; command still decisive cmd6-vs-2 -> 40-0). (2) FIX DISCIPLINE -- discipline_penalty() now continuous over the old tiers' endpoints (disc>=5->0, disc 1->-2), no step. KNOWN TRADE-OFF (Jordan-accepted): the smooth pool reduces FORMATION/charge decisiveness; historical gauge (tests/sim/gauge_mb.py; precedents_warfare.md bands) needs RE-CALIBRATION to the smooth engine -- FLAGGED separate Jordan-canon follow-up. File: tests/sim/mass_battle/orchestration.py. Jordan-vetoable.

title: Recalibrate mass-battle gauge bottom-up from historical + academic grounding; switch win-metric to decisive split
description: Re-derived the mass-battle gauge bands bottom-up from historical precedent + peer-reviewed academic military analysis (NOT fitted to engine output), resolving the ED-1013 gauge-staleness flag. (1) METRIC: raw-A% (draw-contaminated; symmetric mirrors failed on draws) -> DECISIVE SPLIT decA=A/(A+B); draw rate validated separately as a decisiveness dimension (near-parity high-draw is analytically expected: Hillestad/Owen/Blumenthal 1995 NRL 42(2); Taylor 1979/1983 NRLQ; Lanchester tie Armstrong&Sodergren 2015 SSQ 96(4)). (2) GROUNDING: generalship>numbers (Biddle 2004); full envelopment the one reliable geometric edge (Cannae; Sidnell 2006); formation geometry alone weak at equal command (Burkholder 2007); frontal cavalry vs steady foot CONTESTED not a win (Burkholder 2007 -> C1 REBASELINED 52-80->35-55, the old band was the popular misconception); braced foot beats frontal cavalry (Waterloo squares; Barua 2011); cavalry decisive vs shaken/flanked + pursuit (Boddy 2015). (3) VALIDATION (smooth engine, multi, n=120): 9/20 VALIDATED (mirrors; envelopment foot+mounted C4 93.8%/C7 86.7%; command-decisive; maniples-absorb-wedge; misconception-corrected C1 45.7%); 6 DIVERGE-soft (subtle formation edges washed by the ED-1013 cohesion pool, defensible per Biddle/Burkholder but below the v9 A.6 modest edge); 3 DIVERGE-hard ENGINE-DEFECT FLAGS left FAILING not fitted (C2/C6 braced foot never wins -> brace under-repels; C5 morale-shock inert, decA == C1); R1 ranged loses open-field but too-drawish; R3 ranged mirror unresolvable; single-mode all-draws (18-tick cap). Full per-band rationale + DOIs: references/historical/mass_battle_gauge_grounding.md. Engine-defect flags (frontal cavalry shock/brace/morale flat) are future-work targets, not band errors.

system: tests/sim/gauge_mb.py (mass-battle historical gauge) / mass_battle frontal-cavalry resolution
approved_by: Jordan (directive 2026-06-16: 'for 1, 2 and 3 do everything you can to try to fix everything from a bottom-up emergent approach'); implemented by Claude, Jordan-vetoable
title: Fix mass-battle gauge cavalry test-construction (brace instruction + genuine shaken morale_start); the 3 ED-1014 'engine-defect' flags were gauge-construction, engine mechanics already grounded/WIRED
description: Re-diagnosed the 3 ED-1014 DIVERGE-hard cavalry flags (C2/C5/C6) bottom-up by reading the engine resolution code (mass_battle/resolution.py, orchestration.py, config.py); the verdict INVERTS -- they were GAUGE-CONSTRUCTION defects, not engine defects. The engine's frontal-cavalry mechanics are already grounded + WIRED and are UNTOUCHED: (a) the reciprocal brace-recoil (PC_CHARGE_RECOIL=6, calibrated vs Courtrai/Swiss/Waterloo) fires only on the 'brace' instruction (_unit_braced); Phase-2 built 'braced' as hold-stance+disc8 WITHOUT the instruction, so the recoil never fired (square held but never repelled). (b) the shaken-shock amplifier (PC_SHOCK_SHAKEN_GAIN) + _morale_sigma key off morale/morale_start ('shaken' is RELATIVE -- du Picq -- cohesion eroded BELOW start); Phase-2's make_unit forced morale_start==morale, so C5's morale-2 line read 100% relative morale (not shaken). FIX (gauge only): make_unit gains morale_start=None + instructions=() with byte-exact defaults (13 H/R rows + C1/C3/C4/C7 unchanged; H1 identical 52.8); C2/C6 defenders carry instructions=('brace',); C5 is morale 2 of start 6. METRIC: C2/C6 judged on RAW cav-a LOW (band 0-30) with draws expected (a repulse is a HOLD; decisive-split saturates at a tiny decisive n) -- optional 10th tuple field metric='rawA' (default 'decA'). BANDS (history-grounded, Jordan-vetoable): C5 ceiling 90->98 (cavalry vs disordered foot is NEAR-TOTAL -- Boddy 2015 dispersed 15,000; Hastings post-feint; the Phase-2 ceiling was set when the shock was inert); C7 ceiling 90->100 (encirclement of an immobile hold-stance line that cannot turn to face is annihilating -- Cannae/Adrianople; decA saturates to 100 when infantry is shut out). VALIDATION (smooth engine, multi, n=120; C5 re-checked n=240): cavalry block 7/7 -- C1 contested 45.7 OK, C2 REPELLED (raw cav-a 1.7), C3 mirror 43.7 OK, C4 envelop 93.8 OK, C5 shaken 95.6 (94.8@n=240) OK, C6 REPELLED 1.7, C7 envelop 100.0 OK. The differentiation is EMERGENT from the already-grounded mechanics: braced-repulse ~2% != contested ~46% != shaken-shock ~95% != envelopment ~94-100% -- this validates the ED-1014 C1 rebaseline (35-55) by differentiation. gauge_mb.py: 0 uncited constants, mechanics_selftest (True,[]). LATENT ENGINE FLAG (out of scope, not triggered): the charge-recoil (orchestration ~L1647) does not zone-gate, so a flank/rear charge into a braced unit would wrongly fire the recoil; C7 uses hold-only to avoid it -- fix candidate is to gate the recoil on the frontal (GREEN) octagon zone. The 6 formation soft-divergences (H2/H4/H5/H6/H7/H9, smooth-pool wash) remain a separate residual. Supersedes the ED-1014 section-4 'C2/C5/C6 engine-defect, future-work' framing. Full per-band rationale + DOIs: references/historical/mass_battle_gauge_grounding.md.

approved_by: Jordan (directive 2026-06-16: "Unit's Power and HP etc should all be derived from its Subunits given different unit types/troop counts per subunit"; chose per-subunit pool option 1 (each subunit rolls its own pool, shared Command); "Do so with intensive attention to detail")
title: Derive Unit combat stats from Subunits -- per-subunit power/discipline/morale/dr + per-subunit combat pool (shared Command)
description: Pushed the quality stats (power/discipline/morale/morale_start/dr) onto Subunit as OPTIONAL fields (None inherits the parent Unit via a _unit back-ref). Added per-subunit cohesion + module subunit_combat_pool (SHARED Command, per-subunit Discipline+cohesion, shared stamina), and repointed resolve_engagements (pool; Lanchester casualty power/dr), the charge-shock / brace-recoil / morale delta-sigma, and the Phase-2 volley (power/discipline/eff_size) to read the contacting subunit's effective stats. Unit derives troop-weighted aggregates (agg_power/discipline/morale/dr) atop the existing HP=Size=Sum(subunits). Single-subunit fast-paths (cohesion->hp/hp_max, eff_size->effective_size) + None-inheritance make every homogeneous/single-subunit unit byte-exact: VERIFIED vs a clean pre-edit engine across melee/cavalry/brace/morale AND ranged/volley (exact final states + n=30/40 win-rates identical). Mixed-unit differentiation demonstrated (cavalry subunit P6/D6 pool 8 vs infantry subunit P4/D4 pool 7, carried into the combat trace). Added make_mixed_unit gauge constructor (make_unit single-subunit path unchanged). V1 SCOPE: per-subunit combat morale uses the subunit's nominal value for overriding subunits; the eroding morale pool + rout + discipline degradation remain UNIT-level (non-overriding subunits inherit the eroding unit value -> byte-exact). Implemented by Claude, Jordan-vetoable.

approved_by: Jordan (directive 2026-06-17: give each Subunit its OWN stamina (optional, None inherits Unit) so rotation / supporting-troops / depth is justified by historical precedent -- a fresh rear/reserve subunit relieves an exhausted engaged one; depth = stamina reserve (triplex-acies line relief; Clausewitz reserves))
title: Per-subunit stamina -- optional Subunit stamina/stamina_max + per-subunit drain/recovery/pool, byte-exact single-subunit (mirrors ED-1016)
description: Pushed stamina from Unit onto Subunit as OPTIONAL fields (stamina/stamina_max; None inherits the parent Unit via the _unit back-ref). Added eff_stamina/eff_stamina_max (own value else inherit), drain_stamina/recover_stamina (write routing: own-stamina-if-set else the inherited Unit, so a single-subunit unit reproduces the old Unit.stamina arithmetic exactly), and agg_stamina (troop-weighted) for unit-level reads. Repointed to per-subunit: subunit_combat_pool pool penalty (atom.eff_stamina), base_combat_pool (agg_stamina), the per-tick contact drain (each engaged subunit drains by its own cells-in-contact; reserves do not drain), phase-boundary recovery (stamina_check via a new per-subunit _subunit_depth), between-turn recovery, and the exhaustion-pressure morale read. Deliberately did NOT change _fatigue_sigma: that per-column fatigue channel is already sub-unit-scoped at its call site (it is passed the engaged subunit's contact columns), so an atom parameter would be unused apparatus (NERS-E). Extended make_mixed_unit to accept per-subunit stamina/stamina_max. Byte-exact vs a clean pre-edit engine across nine matchups (melee mirror / asymmetric / envelopment, ranged / volley, cavalry charge / braced / envelopment / shaken) by twenty seeds in the resolving multi-turn mode (identical state-vector digest). Rotation demonstrated: an engaged front subunit drained while a held reserve stayed fresh (a divergence impossible under shared Unit.stamina); the fresh reserve yielded a larger combat pool than the exhausted front. Grounding: Sabin 2000 JRS (line relief / supporting troops); Zhmodikov 2000 Historia; du Picq Battle Studies; Clausewitz On War III.12. V1 SCOPE: per-subunit rout + eroding morale/discipline remain UNIT-level (deferred, Jordan-vetoable).

approved_by: Jordan (directive 2026-06-17: wire the troop taxonomy onto the per-subunit stats -- a subunit OF a type should carry that type's canonical combat stats; completes the stat home the 2026-06-16 completeness audit flagged missing)
title: Troop-taxonomy stat home -- TROOP_TYPE_STATS (canonical B.2 presets) + stats_for + Subunit.of_type, byte-exact (additive)
description: Wired the troop taxonomy onto the per-subunit stats added in ED-1016/ED-1017. Added orchestration.TROOP_TYPE_STATS, a preset table transcribing the TTRPG Power/Discipline/Morale columns of the canonical BG unit table (mass_battle_v30.md B.2) keyed to the existing snake_case taxonomy (config.TROOP_TYPE_ROLES): levy, light_infantry, heavy_infantry, cavalry, archers, crossbow, sling, artillery, knights_templar. Placed in orchestration beside its accessor stats_for and constructor of_type (where the role accessors roles_for/role_allowed already live; the role-scaffold data TROOP_TYPE_ROLES/ROLE_SPEC stays in config). Added orchestration.stats_for(troop_type) (case-insensitive accessor mirroring roles_for; returns a fresh dict or None for an unknown type) and the constructor Subunit.of_type(troop_type, shape, tier, starting_position, **kw), which fills power/discipline/morale/morale_start from the preset unless the caller overrides them; an unknown type fills nothing so the fields stay None and inherit the parent Unit. PURELY ADDITIVE: nothing that does not call of_type changes, so the gauge constructors (make_unit/make_mixed_unit) are untouched and single-subunit/homogeneous units stay byte-exact. SCOPE: only the three unambiguous B.2 integers are mapped. dr (the B.2 Armour column) and stamina (the Endur column) are deliberately left to inherit -- Armour maps to a vs-Piercing DR scale (orch L413-417, None/Light/Medium/Heavy) whose identity with the Subunit.dr field is unconfirmed, and Endur has no clean bridge to the 0-100 stamina pool; both flagged for an explicit follow-up rather than guessed. unit_type (melee vs ranged) stays caller-controlled (a role, not a stat). Validation: byte-exact -- the 9-matchup x 20-seed multi-turn battery digest is unchanged from the pre-edit engine (fe99574610caca44052509beb8c0b81a1b3d1972c6a3c8e3513e38933ef27c69). Differentiation demonstrated: of_type reproduces every B.2 row exactly, and the stats separate combat on both channels -- the pool via discipline (Levy 6 dice to Cavalry/Knights Templar 8 dice) and damage via the (1+Power) multiplier (Levy x2 to Cavalry x6); caller overrides beat the preset; unknown types inherit the Unit. Grounding: the canonical B.2 table itself (bottom-up); Sabin Lost Battles 2007 (validated ancient-battle model rating units by type and quality) plus the settled heavy/light/missile distinction (top-down).

system: tests/sim/mass_battle/orchestration.py (mass_battle engine) / per-subunit rout + eroding morale & discipline
approved_by: Jordan (directive 2026-06-17 + 'Continue' confirmation of the recommended design: push rout/morale/discipline onto the Subunit so a section of the line can break from its own casualties while a fresh sibling holds -- unit-rout DERIVED, NO intra-unit cascade)
title: Per-subunit rout + eroding morale/discipline -- write-routed Subunit morale/discipline/rout, unit-rout derived, byte-exact single-subunit (mirrors ED-1017)
description: Pushed rout, morale erosion, and discipline degradation from the Unit onto the Subunit, completing the per-subunit lifecycle begun in ED-1016 (stats) and ED-1017 (stamina). Previously all three were unit-level: morale_check_phase eroded unit.morale from the UNIT Size fraction, rout_resolution routed the unit at unit.morale<=0, discipline_check_phase degraded unit.discipline; a unit broke as one indivisible block. The config's SUBUNIT_ROUT_FLOOR was a dead stub (defined, exported, never referenced) -- the fossil of an intended-but-unbuilt per-subunit rout. New Subunit fields routed/broken/discipline_start; new props/methods eff_discipline_start, erode_morale, degrade_discipline, restore_discipline (all write-routed: own value if set, else the inherited Unit, so single-subunit/homogeneous units reproduce the old unit arithmetic exactly -> byte-exact); new Unit methods derive_rout and cascade_morale_hit. Repointed to per-subunit: morale_check_phase (each subunit erodes by its OWN cohesion + own stamina/discipline exhaustion), rout_resolution (each subunit routs at its own eff_morale<=0, then derive_rout), discipline_check_phase (unit-level loss asymmetry drives a per-subunit degradation counter via eff_discipline_start-eff_discipline), reform_check (flag-gated OFF), the run-loop rout trigger, the per-tick stamina drain (routed subunit skipped), subunit_combat_pool (routed/broken subunit -> 0 dice), and the multi-unit inter-unit cascade / flank-erosion morale hits (via cascade_morale_hit, which hits the unit's inherited-default morale ONCE plus each own-morale subunit -> no double-count on homogeneous units). TWO CANON-STRUCTURE FORKS, both Jordan-vetoable: (a) unit-rout is DERIVED -- the unit routs when its troop-weighted aggregate morale reaches 0 (the canonical Morale-0 rout of A.4/A.12 read as the composite), or all subunits have routed, or the general is gone (Command<=0); winner/pursuit/inter-unit-cascade keep keying on this derived unit.routed, so the spec's unit-level rout model is preserved and per-subunit rout is granularity below it. (b) NO intra-unit cascade was added -- A.12's Morale Cascade stays inter-unit per the spec, even though its own 'one section of the line broke and panic spread' (Cannae, Hastings) rationale could justify a routed subunit pressuring its siblings; that is a canon-model change left for Jordan. NO mass_battle_v30 edit (engine elaboration consistent with the spec's unit-level rout, byte-exact for homogeneous). The unit rout-report keys and the Command<=0 morale-zero line are unchanged (the report reads unit.morale; rerouting through agg_morale risks a float-precision digest divergence -- NERS-E). VALIDATION: byte-exact -- the 9-matchup x 20-seed multi-turn battery digest is unchanged from the pre-edit engine (fe99574610caca44052509beb8c0b81a1b3d1972c6a3c8e3513e38933ef27c69). Per-subunit rout demonstrated: a two-subunit unit (heavy-infantry front + levy rear) with the rear gutted to ~18% eroded the REAR's morale to 0 over two phases (cohesion 0.18 -> -2/phase) while the FRONT stayed at 5.0 (cohesion 1.0 -> 0 loss); rout_resolution routed the rear only (rear.routed=True, front.routed=False, UNIT.routed=False -- the line held), the rear's pool dropped to 0 while the front kept 7 dice, and breaking the front too routed the unit (all subunits routed -> derive_rout); a single-subunit unit routs exactly when its morale hits 0, as before. Grounding (references/historical/mass_battle_gauge_grounding.md S8): A.12's own Cannae/Hastings sectional-collapse rationale; du Picq Battle Studies (panic is local and propagates from a break).

approved_by: Claude (stress-test finding during the ED-1018/1019 NERS audit, Jordan-directed 2026-06-17); Jordan-vetoable
title: Fix per-subunit broken-state scope: a single Discipline-0 sub-unit no longer zeroes its healthy siblings' pools (ED-1019 follow-up)
description: Per-subunit stress testing of ED-1019 caught a defect. subunit_combat_pool set unit.broken=True the moment ANY single sub-unit's eff_discipline hit 0, and its top gate (unit.routed or unit.broken or atom.routed or atom.broken -> return 0) then zeroed EVERY sibling's pool: one broken section silently broke the whole unit's combat ability, an unintended intra-unit break cascade contradicting the canonized A.4 ('a routed/broken sub-unit's siblings fight on') and the A.12 'cascade is inter-unit only' decision (ED-1019, canonized into mass_battle_v30 commit 0f5b2893). The single-subunit byte-exact gauge never exposed it (no siblings); the multi-subunit stress battery did. FIX: the Discipline-0 gate sets atom.broken (that sub-unit's formation gone -> contributes 0) and promotes to unit.broken ONLY when all(s.broken for s in unit.subunits). Single-subunit: lone sub-unit broken => all() true => unit.broken as before (byte-exact). Re-test: digest unchanged; one broken levy pool 0 + healthy heavy sibling fights + unit NOT broken; all broken -> unit.broken; single-subunit disc0 -> unit.broken. Regression test tests/sim/mass_battle/test_persubunit_stress.py (S1-S10, all pass). Found + fixed by Claude, Jordan-vetoable.

approved_by: Jordan
title: D-A wound model: Spirit->WI, Strength->Health, health-based felling (uniformity + 4-6 hits)
description: Jordan 2026-06-18: more noise in Health (Strength at very low weight proportional to Endurance) and Spirit at low weight increasing the Wound Interval, to reduce uniformity; equal average chars fall in ~4-6 hits. WI=round(End+4+0.4*Spirit) (flat base 6->4 reallocated, avg Health held at 40); Health=round(WI*(MW+1)+0.25*Strength*End); felling switched wound-COUNT (>=MW+1 wounds) -> health-depletion (cumulative>=Health) because count-felling made Strength->Health a no-op (str7-v-4 0.877~mirror). Validated MB=50: ~5.2 hits, mirror 0.516, spi7-v-4 0.50->0.81, str7-v-4 ~0.88, noise Health 37/40/43 WI 8/9/11; r2 self-test 7/7. Pre-existing ~50% multi-bout stall noted. Isolated to personal combat.

system: tests/sim/mass_battle/orchestration.py (mass_battle engine) / discipline_check_phase per-subunit loss fidelity
approved_by: Jordan (directive 2026-06-17: 'Address roll-input fidelity item'); Jordan-vetoable
title: Roll-input fidelity: per-subunit Discipline degrades from the sub-unit's OWN cumulative loss, not the whole unit's
description: discipline_check_phase drove every subunit's Discipline degradation from the UNIT's cumulative hp loss (a_loss/b_loss) and the unit-level loss asymmetry, so a fresh reserve subunit cracked from casualties its engaged siblings took -- a roll-input fidelity gap (per-subunit Discipline feeds the per-subunit combat pool penalty but was driven by a unit-level signal). morale_check_phase already reads per-subunit cohesion; this brings Discipline into line. FIX: each subunit's loss = its own (_start_troops - cur_troops)/BLOCK_SIZE; a single-subunit unit uses the exact (hp_max-hp)/BLOCK_SIZE expression (mirrors cohesion's byte-exact fast-path) so the homogeneous gauge is unchanged (digest fe99...c69). Loss-asymmetry baseline stays the opposing UNIT (no per-subunit opponent in the 1v1 engine -- conservative, flagged). Verified: digest unchanged; a gutted subunit degrades from own loss while a fresh reserve sibling (unit-level loss 2.0) does NOT. Regression test test_persubunit_stress.py S11. Found in the ED-1018/1019 NERS audit, fixed by Claude on Jordan's directive; Jordan-vetoable.

system: tests/sim/gauge_mb.py / make_mixed_unit of_type wiring (consumes the ED-1018 taxonomy stat home)
approved_by: Jordan (directive 2026-06-17: 'Wire make_mixed_unit to of_type'); Jordan-vetoable
title: Wire make_mixed_unit to Subunit.of_type so canonical troop types draw their B.2 stat presets (ED-1018 consumed)
description: make_mixed_unit built each subunit via Subunit(...) with explicit per-subunit stats, so the ED-1018 taxonomy stat home (of_type) had no construction-path consumer (the Part-2 wiring gap). FIX: make_mixed_unit now builds each subunit via Subunit.of_type(troop_type, shape, tier, pos, **kw), forwarding ONLY the stat keys the caller explicitly set so of_type's setdefault fills the rest from the B.2 preset; a non-canonical type ('infantry') fills nothing and inherits the unit fallbacks exactly as before, override still wins. Additive: make_mixed_unit has no current callers; bat.py (the byte-exact digest harness) has its own make_unit and is unaffected (digest fe99...c69 unchanged). Verified: py_compile OK; a knights_templar spec draws B.2 5/6/6, override wins, 'infantry' inherits. Regression test S12. Implemented by Claude on Jordan's directive; Jordan-vetoable.

approved_by: Jordan (ruling 2026-06-17: 'fractional-morale should be continuous'); Jordan-vetoable
title: Ruling: Morale is a continuous quantity in play (no integer coercion); confirms existing engine behavior
description: The per-subunit mechanic-audit (Mode B) flagged that the exhaustion sub-term 1/(eff_discipline*command) takes Morale off the canon integer 1-7 scale. Jordan ruled Morale is CONTINUOUS in play: B.2 starting values are integers, but erosion is continuous and Morale is stored/compared as a float. No engine change required -- Morale is already a float throughout (erode_morale, agg_morale, cascade_morale_hit all do float arithmetic); the only rounding is round(u.morale, 3) for the turn-LOG display (not a coercion of the live value), and there is no int(morale) anywhere. This entry records the ruling and resolves the flag. Raised in the per-subunit audit; ruled by Jordan; Jordan-vetoable.

approved_by: Jordan (directive 2026-06-17: 'subunit morale reset at campaign boundary'); Jordan-vetoable
title: Add reset_morale_between_battles: per-subunit + unit Morale reset at the campaign boundary (PP-711); Discipline persists (PP-712)
description: The engine had no between-BATTLE reset: between_turn_recovery is the within-battle turn boundary (BETWEEN_TURN_MORALE_RECOVERY=0, so Morale persists turn-to-turn within a battle -- correct). PP-711 (Morale resets between battles) was unimplemented for the campaign boundary, and per-subunit su.morale had no reset path (the flag from the audit). FIX: new reset_morale_between_battles(unit) resets unit.morale and each own-Morale subunit's su.morale to its nominal start (eff_morale_start), and clears routed/broken (rout is DERIVED from Morale<=0, so resetting Morale without clearing the sticky flags -- derive_rout only SETS -- would leave an incoherent routed-but-full unit). Discipline is NOT reset (PP-712 persists). Inherited-Morale subunits are covered by the unit reset. Called by the campaign layer at the battle boundary, NOT within a single battle -> new uncalled function, byte-exact (digest fe99...c69 unchanged). Exported in __all__. Regression test S13. Implemented by Claude on Jordan's directive; Jordan-vetoable.

approved_by: Jordan (directive 2026-06-17: 'fix both' -- the two per-subunit-Discipline fidelity gaps from the integration sweep); Jordan-vetoable
title: Extend per-subunit Discipline to formation handling: advance_cells movement-hold + check_drift read the subunit's own Discipline
description: Integration-sweep findings 1-2: two formation paths read UNIT Discipline where per-subunit is faithful, inconsistent with the per-subunit Discipline model (cf. the combat-pool fidelity fix). (1) run_battle passed unit.discipline to each atom.advance_cells, so every subunit used the UNIT's Discipline for its movement formation-hold (the disc_mult speed gate) -- now atom.eff_discipline, so a low-discipline subunit advances slower than a high-discipline sibling. (2) Unit.check_drift judged per-subunit-shape drift on self.discipline -- now a.eff_discipline, so a subunit whose OWN Discipline is below its shape minimum drifts to Line while a disciplined sibling keeps its shape. Both byte-exact for a single-subunit unit (the lone subunit inherits -> eff_discipline == unit.discipline; digest fe99...c69 unchanged). Regression tests S14 (drift) + S15 (advance, incl. a shared-Discipline contrast showing the multi-subunit behavior change). Found in the sweep, fixed on Jordan's 'fix both' directive; Jordan-vetoable.

system: tests/sim/mass_battle/orchestration.py / recalc_size, run_multi_unit_battle cascade, between_turn_recovery, discipline_penalty_volley
approved_by: Jordan (directive 2026-06-17: 'address all findings' -- remaining integration-sweep findings 3-6); Jordan-vetoable
title: Close integration-sweep findings 3-6: rout propagation on destruction, per-subunit-aware cascade denominator, per-subunit between-turn Morale recovery, delete dead volley penalty
description: Findings 3-6 from the integration sweep, all byte-exact for the single-subunit gauge (digest fe99...c69 unchanged). (3) recalc_size set unit.routed on size-0 destruction without marking subunits -> now propagates a.routed=True to every subunit so per-subunit consumers see the rout (the unit-level pool gate already covered the pool; this fixes consistency). (4) run_multi_unit_battle's inter-unit morale-cascade denominator used the nominal unit.discipline -> now target.agg_discipline() (troop-weighted per-subunit mean; == unit.discipline when homogeneous), so a mixed unit's cascade resistance reflects its actual composition. (6) between_turn_recovery recovered Morale only at the unit level -> now also recovers each own-Morale subunit toward its start (inert at BETWEEN_TURN_MORALE_RECOVERY=0; correct if ever enabled). (5) deleted discipline_penalty_volley -- dead code, zero call sites (the volley pool uses the per-subunit fire path, shooter_atom.eff_discipline). Regression tests S16 (rout propagation), S17 (agg_discipline denominator), S18 (per-subunit recovery). Found in the sweep, addressed on Jordan's 'address all findings' directive; Jordan-vetoable.

system: designs/provincial/mass_battle_v30.md (canon) / per-sub-unit mass-battle model v4.10 canonization
approved_by: Jordan (directive 2026-06-17: 'canonize all'); Jordan-vetoable
title: Canonize the per-sub-unit mass-battle refinements into mass_battle_v30.md (v4.10): ED-1020/1022/1024/1025/1026/1027
description: Updated the canonical design doc to match the validated engine, extending the v4.9 per-sub-unit addendum (ED-1018/1019) with: ED-1020 (a sub-unit breaks independently at Discipline 0; the unit breaks only when ALL sub-units break) and ED-1022 (each sub-unit's Discipline degrades from its OWN cumulative Size loss, not the unit's) in §A.4; ED-1024 (Morale is continuous in play -- integer starting values, fractional erosion, never rounded) in §A.4; ED-1026 (a sub-unit's own Discipline governs its formation movement-hold and formation-drift) in §A.4; ED-1027 (destruction propagates rout to sub-units; within-battle recovery is per sub-unit; the inter-unit cascade resists by troop-weighted aggregate Discipline) in §A.4 and §A.12; and ED-1025 (the campaign-boundary Morale reset applies per sub-unit and to the unit, clears the derived routed/broken flags, Discipline persists per PP-712) in §A.13. Header bumped v4.9 -> v4.10. Doc-only; the engine and the individual ledger entries are unchanged. ED-1023 (make_mixed_unit->of_type) is gauge construction plumbing, not a canon mechanic -- the §B.2 presets it draws are already canonical. Canonised on Jordan's directive; Jordan-vetoable.

approved_by: Jordan (directive 2026-06-19 'adopt all, ratify, canonize; mark stale/deprecated as required')
title: Combat decision docket 2026-06-17 ratified (adopt-all) — D1-D9 dispositions + Sec-C go
description: Jordan adopt-all directive (2026-06-19) ratifies the consolidated combat decision docket (designs/audit/2026-06-17-combat-decision-docket). Dispositions per docket §B/§C prior recommendations. description: On formation drift to Line (Unit.check_drift), cell_troops is not re-keyed to the new shape's pattern; ~42% of troops (157/376 in test) become spatially orphaned -- counted in HP (sum(cell_troops)==hp holds, so strength/pool correct) but invisible to iter_cells AND inert to front-cell casualty distribution. Fix designed+validated (re-key on drift mirroring spawn: orphan->0, HP preserved); mechanics-touching (alters drifting-unit footprint, engine SHA re-pin required) so adoption pending Jordan. | RESOLVED 2026-06-20: re-key fix committed to tests/sim/mass_battle/orchestration.py check_drift (46373da8). Validated orphan->0, HP preserved, stress S1-S18 pass; gauge digest fe995746->1f8c05a9 (drift-only, non-drift byte-exact). Jordan-approved adoption.

Indirect orientation has no track-movement path. Simulation 2026-06-18 (n=30k randomized, L1 contest): an Indirect-oriented orator reaches a decisive Persuasion Track win 1.8% vs 31.9% Direct, because params/contest.md grants an Indirect win zero track movement (Suspicion Token only). The Indirect archetype (Bury Precedent / Imply Consequence) has no outright-win path. Mechanical-tier, INTENT UNDETERMINED: Jordan to confirm Indirect as a denial/tempo role with a signposted Suspicion-Token payoff, or grant it a track-movement lever. Source: sc_sim Component B. Files under W4 #15 / J-31.

L3 venue docstring/code numeric drift (modes.py, 2026-06-03-contest-groundup): excommunication_court_venue docstring 'High ProofBar (5.0)' vs code ProofBar(bar=3.0); imperial_petition_venue docstring 'GraceThreshold(8.0)' vs code GraceThreshold(bar=5.5). Code authoritative ([SEED] docstrings drifted from prior values). Docstrings reconciled to live code values this session. The excommunication bar VALUE (keep 3.0 vs soften to 2.5) remains D-5(b) / Jordan.

Design rule (adopted as principle): overturn-difficulty bound to recoverability. A venue may carry a hard overturn bar only if its loss is (a) non-lethal/revocable AND (b) the recovery or upstream agency is implemented and legible. Bottom-up: social_contest_v30 7.3 (Heresy Investigation Lifecycle, ED-772) shows excommunication is a multi-season lifecycle with off-ramps (Parliamentary Stay, Inquisitor reassignment/death, coalitional defense, defection) and a revocable terminus, NOT lethal. Top-down: irreversible outcomes gated behind sustained risk or recovery, never a single adverse contest (permadeath-roguelike design). Maps to resolution-diagnostic Lesson 3 + Phase-2b reversibility + Phase-5 intent-gate. No venue currently violates it; stated as principle + new-venue checklist, NOT an enforced engine field (over-engineering guard). Re: directive 2026-06-18.

L3 venue policy-dominance / legibility. Simulation 2026-06-18 (optimal-vs-worst policy sweep, 13 venues): every venue has a worst challenger policy that wins 0.00 (self-clinching/off-ground policies invalid in that venue); policy-mismatch dominates faculty. The L3 analogue of L1 orientation-dominance: the wrong rhetorical approach loses regardless of skill or stats. Legibility risk: skilled play is gated behind knowing venue-to-rhetorical-fit. Mechanical-tier; pairs with a UI venue-to-rhetorical-fit affordance (Godot). Jordan to confirm the implementation surfaces this. Source: sc_sim optimal-policy re-test.

ED-884 mu-shift propagated to contest engine (commit 060f0838). The 06-03 contest engine.py/resolver.py applied sigma-leverage as a clamped Ob-reduction (effective_ob floored at OB_MIN=1), reintroducing 1/sqrt(N) impact non-uniformity (the F1 reopening; ED-1009 module-13 R-basis) above faculty ~3. Fix mirrors ED-884 resolution + ED-934 combat: engine.net_boost mu-shift added, effective_ob DISPLAY-ONLY, resolver._reception switched (net += net_boost; base_Ob/TN unchanged, Ob floor never breached). Validated: z-shift uniform (= eff_sigma) across all pools; 151/151 contest tests pass; venue distributions stable; faculty-advantaged overturn restored (excommunication +4 faculty 0.54->0.72). ED-1009 module-13 leverage-non-uniformity R-defect thereby CLOSED; whole-graph non-conformant count unchanged (other module-13 items remain).

description: Verified bottom-up [READ: orchestration.py L314, PC_ENVELOP_SHOCK, _envelopment_sigma]: the -3 morale-loss cap (L314 min(loss,3.0)) is unconditional; PP-683's encirclement cap-removal is unwired. Wiring it would DOUBLE-COUNT -- the engine delivers the encirclement effect via PC_ENVELOP_SHOCK (du Picq moral shock; a loose/shaken/shallow unit shatters, Cannae) + the Lanchester contact-overlap edge, and a second encirclement term breaks H4 Cannae, the same NERS-N/E reason _envelopment_sigma is held dormant. approved_by: Jordan
title: Combat impact model reverted: M-STR multiplicative -> additive (strength+heft)
description: core.py damage() impact reverted from commit 2a2c9f78's multiplicative M-STR (impact=(STR_REF+heft)*(strength/STR_REF)^STR_EXP) to additive impact=strength+heft, per damage_model.py's 2026-05-30 design. Resolved by full-mechanics sim v33-mstr-impact: multiplicative (mstr_lin) stalled low-strength heavy-weapon fights (str2 greatsword mirror 42% draws vs 7% additive) for <=3pt win-rate gain; additive resolves cleanly across the strength range (str7/str4/str2 greatsword mirror = 11.4/14.1/19.4 landed hits to fell; mirrors fair). STR_REF/STR_EXP removed as dead; M-QUAL (sigma-leverage tail) untouched; docstring already specified strength+heft so doc/code agreement restored. CORRECTION: the ~50% draw 'stall' and ~97% first-mover 'A-bias' reported across the 2026-06-19 session were artifacts of the sim harness misreading fight()'s -1/0/+1 return as a 0/1 loser-index; with correct attribution the engine is fair and resolves ~98% of fights. This MOOTS the prior §4 attack/defense asymmetry-fix item, which was premised on that phantom stall. Resolves the D1 impact-model fork; the full continuous-transmission D1 adoption (armour_axes + damage_model coupling) remains a separate queued build.

approved_by: Jordan
title: Combat wounds: bilateral Ob channel (+0.15 atk / +0.25 def per wound) replaces -1D aggressor-only pool penalty
description: Combat wounds switched from a -1D aggressor-only dice-pool penalty (ED-1021) to a bilateral obstacle (Ob) channel: each wound adds +0.15 Ob when the wounded fighter attacks and +0.25 Ob when they defend (defence ~1.6x, per Jordan directive 2026-06-22). RATIONALE: the old -1D pool penalty could only impair offence because the engine has the attacker roll a pool while the defender's skill is baked into net_sigma (no defender pool to dock); a wound impairing defence therefore REQUIRES an Ob/sigma channel. Applied in wrapper.py net_sigma + stop-hit nsig; magnitudes in config.py WOUND_ATK_OB/WOUND_DEF_OB (tunable). VALIDATED: resolution-level P(hit) -5.7pp (1 wound attacking) / +9.0pp (1 wound defending); fight-level a 1-wound fighter's win-rate 15.1->10.3%, mirror stays ~50 (fair). Source commit ab4e550c. INTEGRATION FLAG (future weapon/combat work): (1) extend the wound Ob to the bind resolution (bind_sigma) for full consistency - currently net_sigma + stop-hit only; (2) reconcile with the pending graduated-wound redesign (N=4 wounds, WI=round(2.5*End+1.5*Str+1.0*Spi)) - the Ob penalty (how a wound degrades) and the wound-count model (how many wounds) are orthogonal but must be designed together; (3) magnitudes 0.15/0.25 tunable - revisit in weapon/combat balance passes; (4) ED-1021's -1D pool penalty is superseded and r8.WoundTracker.pool_penalty() is now unused (cleanup candidate); (5) GENERAL PATTERN: continuous Ob is the engine's channel for any defender-side condition (prone, surprised, flanked, terrain) since the defender has no pool - apply this template to future conditions rather than a pool penalty.
citations: ED-1040 (additive impact, the engine this builds on)

approved_by: PENDING (Jordan canon-structure decision)
title: Wound-model drift: code implements E+Str+Spi interval + N=4 wounds; canonical derived_stats_v30 §4.1 says Endurance-only interval + max 3
description: Surfaced during the ED-1041 wound-Ob work. The implemented wound model (r2_consequence_wounds.py, imported via r8 into the canonical engine) ALREADY realizes the 'pending graduated-wound redesign': wound_interval = round(2.6*End + 1.6*Str + 1.0*Spi) (~ the proposed 2.5/1.5/1.0) with N_WOUNDS=4 graduated levels, health_full = WI*N_WOUNDS. BUT the canonical constants in the SAME file, tagged [canonical: derived_stats_v30 §4.1], contradict it: WOUND_INTERVAL_BASE=6 (Endurance-ONLY interval), MAXWOUNDS_DIV=2 / MAXWOUNDS_ADD=1 / MAXWOUNDS_CAP=3 (max 3 wounds, floor(End/2)+1). So the running code uses an Endurance+Strength+Spirit interval with 4 wound levels; the canonical spec says Endurance-only interval with 3 max wounds. These cannot both be canon. RECOMMENDATION (mechanical-tier analysis, Jordan to ratify the canon decision): adopt the IMPLEMENTED model as canonical -- bottom-up it is what the validated engine already runs; top-down an interval scaling with endurance, frame/strength, and will/spirit is better-grounded than endurance alone, and graduated multi-level wound tracks are standard injury modeling. If ratified: (a) update derived_stats_v30 §4.1 to the E+Str+Spi formula + N=4; (b) remove the vestigial WOUND_INTERVAL_BASE / MAXWOUNDS_DIV / MAXWOUNDS_ADD / MAXWOUNDS_CAP constants; (c) ED-1041 item-4 cleanup -- delete the now-dead WoundTracker.pool_penalty() and its stale '-1D per wound, no Ob channel (canon universality)' comment (wounds are an Ob channel as of ED-1041). ALTERNATIVE: if canonical intent is Endurance-only + cap-3, revert the code constants instead. Flagged for Jordan rather than changed unilaterally: altering the authored derived_stats_v30 §4.1 formula would be a retcon, which is barred without ratification.
stale_reason: Superseded — Wound model reconciled 2026-06-18 via ED-1021 (D-A): sim r2_consequence_wounds.py & derived_stats_v30 §4.1 share WI=round(End+4+0.4·Spi); drift gone.

Mass-battle bottom-up re-architecture audit + staged/gated/adversarial roadmap. Findings: engine.py is an inert re-exporter while orchestration.py (2,899 L) is a god-file conflating data model + targeting + attrition-law authoring + volley + resolution + turn loop + I/O; troop-types/formations/tactics/strategy exist only as inert data; 9 anti-patterns (8 isolated to geometry.py/config.py), dominated by shape-name lookup (cell_speed, four *_cells tables = ungrounded) and ~20 calibrated-to-band PC_* magnitudes. Proposes a full clean module split (wrapper resolves nothing / core resolves only / every module wired), a derive-from-primitive constant bar (history validates behaviour, never licenses a value; calibrated = debt -> 0), a GRANULARITY dial for unit/subunit/cell deployment, and a 7-gate per-stage sequence (code-arch audit + evidentiary-primitive audit + adversarial red-team + top-down gauge + byte-exact). Audit pass is read-only + the additive provenance.py seed; Stages 1-6 gated for separate approval. Jordan-vetoable.

Combat parity oracle contradiction (ecosystem review #1.1, CRITICAL). designs/scene/combat_engine_v1/config.py ADEF_THRESHOLD={light:0.70,medium:0.45,heavy:0.72} is non-monotonic (medium<light); the Godot port designs/godot/skeleton/.../combat_config.gd diverged in-place to monotonic {light:0.45,medium:0.60,heavy:0.72} via an [AUDIT-FIX] note that itself states 'a parity re-sweep is required before this ships'. config.py is Class-C (harness-calibrated, not canon), so the corrected values are a balance/re-calibration decision, not a transcription fix. While the two disagree, the Key-log replay parity gate cannot go green. ACTION: re-sweep the harness to set monotonic ADEF_THRESHOLD in config.py, then regenerate the .tres/.gd from it; never let the port correct its oracle in-place. RESOLVED 2026-06-30 (Jordan: fix monotone + re-sweep): config.ADEF_THRESHOLD re-swept to MONOTONE {light:0.30, medium:0.45, heavy:0.72} (grounded frame: a gambeson is soft/easily-defeated, mail harder, plate hardest; light 0.70->0.30 fixes the backwards inversion). combat_config.gd RE-EXPORTED from the oracle, retiring the port's private [AUDIT-FIX] in-place correction (CLAUDE.md §6 closed for adef_threshold); adef_point 1.0->1.2 also re-exported. Validated: 78 tests, mirror-50, light matchups sane. The fuller .gd module re-export (RESIST/GAP_EXPOSURE/the gap-game logic) remains the deferred module port behind the non-compilable skeleton.

Godot module-contract coverage gap (ecosystem review #1.2/#1.3). references/module_contracts.yaml has 10/27 modules with doc:null (no home design doc, including engine_clock the temporal spine) and 11/27 resolvers at [ASSUMPTION] grade (determinism class unconfirmed for a no-GM engine). The Gate-0 spine (BaseEngine/EngineModule/Key/KeyBus/GameState/Resolver) is referenced by the skeleton but defined nowhere. ACTION: author the doc:null modules (engine_clock first) and adjudicate each [ASSUMPTION] resolver before the port consumes it; land Gate-0 as compilable GDScript with the dice kernel green against references/d10_success_probabilities.json.

Numeric layer is non-ingestible for Godot (ecosystem review #2.1/#2.2/#2.4). All mechanical params live as prose markdown in params/*.md; references/values_master.yaml stores formulas as byte-identical free-text English (not ASTs), indexes a nonexistent params/combat.md (~70 entries) and pulls 8 values from params/threadwork_superseded.md; descriptor_registry.yaml marks the attribute roster IN FLUX and Combat Pool is defined three different ways. ACTION: author a typed engine-params file (numeric operands, structured formulas, explicit clamps) as the Godot ingestion source, generated-from-prose and CI round-trip-checked; collapse the Combat Pool triple-definition; gate extract_values freshness.

Integrity gates and sim oracle do not actually validate (ecosystem review #2.3/#3.1/#3.2/#4.1-4.3). tools/freshness_gate.py, broken_dependency_checker.py and patch_propagation_checker.py fetch remote main and require GITHUB_PAT, so they validate main not the diff; the 131 canonical_sha pins are never checked against the working tree; ci_sim_fabrication_check.py whitelists constants by bare integer value globally and splits floats into integer tokens (a fabricated float passes); the ~11.4k-LOC sim/ reference has zero tests and is never run in CI (a seeded batch yields a degenerate win-share nothing flags). ACTION: port the three gates to the local checkout (git hash-object / os.walk, drop the PAT), match fabrication entries by (sim_variable,value,file) and tokenize decimals, add a deterministic seeded sim regression suite to CI.
decision: Ported the three integrity gates (broken_dependency_checker, patch_propagation_checker, freshness_gate) off the GitHub API to the working tree (no GITHUB_PAT/network); freshness now computes git blob SHAs locally and verifies 119/131 canonical_sha pins against the checkout (12 stale pins surfaced as report-only debt). Hardened ci_sim_fabrication_check: full float-literal capture + (variable,value) matching close the §4.1/§4.2 holes. Added the first sim/ test: sim/tests/test_mc_v18_regression.py pins a deterministic seeded batch, wired into a new 'Sim Reference Regression' CI job. Residual (separate, small): refresh the 12 stale pins via freshness_gate --update, then flip freshness from report-only to blocking.

[Filed as ED-1055; renumbered to ED-1080 on merge with main -- contest_rebuild formally reserved 1055-1079 (PR #42).] Grounded weapon x armour x use-mode combat re-baseline (Gate-1: the percussion single-source defect grew, Jordan-directed, into a full evidence-grounded model). BUILT + validated + committed on design/scene-combat-v1 (250eefd7..e4da1f04); PROVISIONAL pending ratify+merge and the [SIM-CALIBRATE] fits. (1) Concussion single-source: core.p_auth DELETED, unified onto weapon_physics.percussion_authority with a 2H/long-lever energy_credit -- A_HANDS=0.25 GROUNDED (measured 2H/1H force ratio ~1.25, Oh et al. 2022 S1), B_ARC=0.04 [SIM-CALIBRATE] (Nathan 2003 bat-MOI penalty); mace 7.45/poleaxe 5.83/staff 2.51 (the poleaxe a weaker pure percussor than a head-heavy mace is the honest outcome). (2) Armour RESIST grounded to Alan Williams, The Knight and the Blast Furnace (2003): 4 cloth/mail cells; the cloth fractions are a DESIGNER normalisation of Williams' joules onto the 0-1 scale -- flagged designer-set/unsourced, NOT a Williams figure; plate.percussion .30 is a package with the FIX-1b authority gate. (3) PRIMITIVE-EMERGENT use-mode selection (systems.afforded_heads/select_mode): a weapon's afforded modes + effectiveness DERIVE from its geometry primitives, retiring the head-collapse -- NO per-weapon mode table (poleaxe/mace/staff are primitive bundles). (4) Situational gap game: a thrust seeks gaps, plate-defeat = max(through-material, GAP_EXPOSURE[mat]*gap_precision) [SIM-CALIBRATE, reach-ladder frame] -- the poleaxe spikes the reach-ladder vs plate, the rondel is the armour-gap weapon, the mace hammers (no point), the staff is weak; all emergent from the derived gap_precision. (5) adef-consistency: ADEF_POINT 1.0->1.2 so a selected gap-thrust's exchange-control matches its damage. Validated: 78 pytest, mirror~50, no one-shot, ranking poleaxe>mace>staff vs plate. Methodology (memory combat-grounding-methodology): derive constants from physics/biomechanics/treatises/materials + adversarially fact-check (4 workflows), never pick or floor; behaviour emerges from primitives. Docs: designs/audit/2026-06-30-combat-grounding/{grounded_weapon_armour_usemode_model,forward_roadmap}.md + designs/audit/2026-06-30-scene-combat-gate1-audit/gate1_audit_report.md. RESIDUAL (deferred): B_ARC/GAP_EXPOSURE/ADEF_POINT magnitudes are fit-not-pinned [SIM-CALIBRATE]; ADEF_THRESHOLD non-monotonicity (ED-1050); the Track-2 single-source purge (agility FIAT clamp, wt/spd legacy reads, WP.reach()/authority() parallel derivations).

Contest rebuild Stage 1d / Gate A: CR1/CR2/CR3 fold-in into canon (prose+params) + D0-3 HYBRID substrate confirmation. CR1 (wrapper->modules 'container' architecture): CONFIRMED already realized by the Stage-1c wrapper (sim/personal/contest/wrapper.py build_contest adapter + resolve_contest router, RESOLVES NOTHING, mirrors tests/sim/mass_battle/engine.py); cited in social_contest_v30 §8 + params/contest.md, not re-built. CR2 (substrate migration: success-counting -> shared sigma-space net engine, per-die -1/0/+1/+2, TN7): CONFIRMED realized Stage 1a/1b, single-sourced onto sim/autoload/sigma_leverage.py (D0-2). CR2 governs the resolution substrate ('what the roll is'); CR6 (leverage-as-delta-sigma, tanh soft-capped, +0.191 uniform across 5D-26D) is the leverage-accumulation half ('how setup advantages enter the roll') -- the two are the same migration, distinct facets, both already realized. D0-3 HYBRID (present-as-Ob, compute-as-delta-sigma): contest stays delta-sigma at TN7 (CR6 upheld, NOT reopened); fractional-Ob rejected as a resolution substrate (re-imports the F1 non-uniformity, verified 2.86-2.94x pool swing vs delta-sigma spread 0.000000) but ADOPTED display-only via the already-DISPLAY-ONLY effective_ob surface (ED-884/ED-934), reproducing the mu-shift bit-identically (err 0.0e+00). This files the ED-1055 that designs/audit/2026-06-30-contest-fractional-ob-probe/MEMO.md proposed but never appended. CR3 fold-in: see ED-1056 (three-tracker model / Face primitive). Concentration formula chain cited throughout: ED-901 (STRUCK Focus*3) + ED-902 ((3*Focus)+(2*Spirit) coefficients + Cognition->Focus engine fix) + ED-933 (params propagation). Out of scope / still-pending: the dice_engine roll_pool TN-fidelity bug (MEMO finding 2a, proposed as a separate ED+PP) is a COMBAT concern (contest is TN7-only, unaffected) and is NOT filed from the contest_rebuild block -- track separately in the combat re-point. Provisional pending Jordan Gate-A ratification (uncommitted worktree diff). [RATIFIED 2026-07-01 (Jordan, Gate B commit): status flipped provisional -> ratified / needs_jordan:false. This corrects a metadata gap from the Gate-A commit -- Jordan ratified all three Gate-A decisions (Face combo formula, scoped Composure retirement, non-basis-citation policy) via AskUserQuestion before Gate A was committed, but the ledger metadata was never updated to reflect it.]

Contest rebuild Stage 1d / Gate A: CR3 three-tracker model folded into canon + Face established as a NAMED tracked primitive + test surface in the sim kernel, AND (2026-07-01 update) the Face scale-binding RESOLVED by Jordan at Gate A (scope-honest -- see below; NOT a fully-realized two-sided resource). CR3 (RATIFIED_2026-06-01): the single Composure buffer is RETIRED as the social-contest tracker and split into its two conflated roles -- Concentration (stamina; (3*Focus)+(2*Spirit), range 5-35, per the ED-901/ED-902/ED-933 chain) + Face (contest-local ethos/standing; v30-surface magnitude Charisma*3, range 3-21 -- the retired Composure magnitude re-homed as TRANSIENT standing). Persuasion Track (merits clock, 0-10 banded) preserved. Face != Disposition/Reputation (transient vs persistent). FACE SCALE-BINDING -- RESOLVED (Jordan, 2026-07-01): a combo formula, not a straight rescale either direction. Face_max = Charisma x 3 (UNCHANGED v30-surface ceiling formula, range 3-21, the retired Composure magnitude) -- a build-time attribute, in player control. Face_current = round(Standing / 10 x Face_max) -- Standing (the kernel 0-10 ethos-built value, earned through play, NOT directly in player control moment-to-moment) determines POSITION WITHIN that ceiling. Standing itself (its internal 0-10 representation, its ethos-build mechanics via .build()/.strip(), and its existing feed into Readiness/leak) is NOT changed by this resolution -- only a derived Face_current accessor is ADDED on top of it, preserving all currently-tested Standing/Readiness behaviour. No new invented constants: reuses the existing Cha x3 formula and the existing 0-10 Standing range. Implemented as sim/personal/contest/primitives.py FaceScale.face_max/FaceScale.face_current (pure functions; Charisma is NOT kernel state, so this is a derived VIEW, not new mutable state) + sim/personal/contest/resolver.py Contestant.charisma (optional build-time field) + _Side.face_max()/_Side.face_current(). COMPOSURE-RETIREMENT BLAST RADIUS -- CONFIRMED (Jordan, 2026-07-01): (a) scoped-rename-only, already correctly implemented -- confirmed correct, no change made; knots/combat/conviction Composure references remain untouched (see social_contest_v30 section 8, social_contest_v30_infill section 4 Knot-as-Composure-buffer note). PROVISIONAL-ED CITABILITY POLICY -- CONFIRMED (Jordan, 2026-07-01): (a) keep non-basis citation phrasing until an ED is ratified, already correctly implemented -- confirmed correct, no change made. PROSE: social_contest_v30 section 4 Step 6 (three-tracker table, now citing the resolved Face_max/Face_current formula; FACE SCALE-BINDING RESOLVED note replacing the prior TWO REPRESENTATIONS open note; honesty note that the strip/strain-consumption channel is still NOT wired this pass) + section 8 (derived-values Face row updated to the resolved formula + CR3 Face scope-honest note updated + Composure-retirement blast-radius note marked RESOLVED) + section 12 ED-127 row + coalition Face row (both re-cited ED-1056); social_contest_v30_infill section 4 (Knot-as-buffer -> Face, re-cited ED-1056, OPEN ITEM marked CONFIRMED); params/contest.md section Derived Values (resolved formula) + section Face and Rattled (FACE SCALE-BINDING RESOLVED note, status corrected to [UNREACHABLE UNTIL STAGE 3] since the strip/strain channel remains unwired) + section CR1/CR2 Substrate CR3 row + PP-255 tiebreak row (both re-cited ED-1056). CODE: Face established as a canonical NAME + registry + test surface in sim/personal/contest/ -- Face = Standing (canonical same-Python-class alias over the existing groundup Standing primitive, NOT a new mechanic and NOT special-cased; the underlying Standing IS live in resolution: built by ethos moves, feeds Readiness+leak); TRACKERS registry {Face->Standing, Concentration->Reserve, PersuasionTrack->adv} + RETIRED_TRACKERS=('Composure',); _Side.face/_Side.concentration accessors; wrapper MECHANICS rows face_tracker+three_trackers=WIRED (the WIRED status vouches for the live-Standing half ONLY); NEW FaceScale.face_max/face_current + _Side.face_max()/_Side.face_current() (Gate-A resolution). NITS FIXED (2026-07-01, all 4 previously-deferred non-gating items from the Gate-A packet): (1) resolver.py dropped the 3 dead imports (Face, TRACKERS, RETIRED_TRACKERS) -- resolver.py now imports FaceScale instead, which it genuinely uses; (2) social_contest_v30.md + params/contest.md CR3/three-tracker/Face rows re-cited from ED-1055 to ED-1056 (this ED) throughout; (3) social_contest_v30.md line-199-ish 'built and stripped' language reworded -- Face_current is now a real, well-defined value (Standing-derived, Charisma-scaled) but strain does NOT yet consume it; the strip/strain-consumption channel stays an honestly-flagged near-term gap (CR5, Stage 3), not silently claimed as wired; (4) primitives.py TRACKERS Concentration row source softened to name Reserve as a [SEED] abstract per-move pool (MAX=12, per-move COST), not a hard-coded (3*Focus)+(2*Spirit) formula claim -- that magnitude is carried by params/contest.md + wrapper.py, not the Reserve class. 234 pre-existing kernel checks remain green; a further +10 kernel checks were ADDED (2026-07-01, closing the Gate-A must-fix coverage gap) directly exercising FaceScale.face_max/face_current and the _Side.face_max()/face_current() accessors -- including the two boundary cases explicitly called for (Standing=0 -> Face_current=0; Standing=Standing.HI -> Face_current=Face_max), the pinned round-half-to-even rounding rule at the midpoint, non-mutation of the underlying Standing.v when reading the derived accessors, and live tracking through an ethos-building move. Kernel total is now 244 (sim/personal/contest/_kernel_tests.py; sim/tests/test_contest_kernel.py _KERNEL_EXPECTED updated 234->244 to match). SCOPE HONESTY (still true after this resolution): Standing.strip() is NEVER called in the contest kernel, so Face has NO strip/strain channel and is monotonic-up; the prose 'strain -> Rattled -> -1D Argue pool' behaviour is a v30-surface spec NOT realized in code; and the RATIFIED_2026-06-01 omega line-20 stamina/standing/merits two-sided tradeoff (+ CR5 F7 self-gating / Face-attack / self-backfire / fallacy-as-foul) is NOT realized here (Stage 3 rhetoric-armature scope). Gate A resolves the SCALE formula only, not the strip/strain-consumption wiring. GATE A STATUS: all 3 open decisions from the Gate-A ratification packet (designs/audit/2026-07-01-contest-gate-a-packet/GATE_A_packet.md) are now RESOLVED by Jordan (2026-07-01): (1) Face scale-binding -- combo formula per above; (2) Composure-retirement blast radius -- scoped-rename-only confirmed; (3) provisional-ED citability -- non-basis phrasing confirmed. Still provisional pending final Jordan sign-off / commit of this worktree diff (no git write commands run by the implementing agent). description: Contest rebuild Stage 2 / Gate B: ED-137 (Panel adjudicator mechanics not yet designed -- use Expert Judge as provisional) CLOSED. Panel is now DESIGNED as a bench of individual judges deliberating to a terminal per-member ballot, realized by the promoted groundup VoteAtClose win-condition (sim/personal/contest/resolver.py). Mechanism: the Persuasion-Track advancement is the room's MOMENTUM (drives each juror's lean); the VERDICT is a SEPARATE terminal secret ballot at close -- each juror votes A iff sharpness*(adv_A - adv_B) + per-juror-noise > 0, so a juror can cross against the room (a lopsided room is near-unanimous, a close room is near a coin-flip). The Panel character/discipline still drives the running momentum via the bench mean (contract.Panel.character()/discipline aggregate the members), so BOTH momentum and the terminal deliberative ballot are member-derived -- this is real promoted mechanics, not a re-skin of Expert Judge. AGGREGATION RULE -- the one genuine judgment call the ratified corpus does not settle: implemented as SIMPLE MAJORITY (>half the ballots; tie -> draw), the best-grounded option (the natural peer-bench default the corpus leaves UNDECIDED -- section 10 (v30:539) is a Mandate-WEIGHTED dice pool banded on the Persuasion Track and section 7.2 (v30:351) is SEVEN heterogeneous rules (Mandate-weighted / quorum / Disposition / seniority / Favour-weighted / consensus), so the corpus argues AGAINST a standing WEIGHT on un-ranked PEER jurors but fixes no peer-bench rule; the one plain-majority-of-individuals analogue is the section 7.2 Crown 'inner circle by majority Disposition'. Needs no invented per-juror standing attribute; keeps Panel non-dominated vs a single Expert Judge). Two alternatives sketched + easy to swap at dictionaries.panel_win_condition(): weighted-by-standing (would need a per-juror standing attribute the Panel type does not yet carry) and unanimity-required (would make Panel strictly harder to win than Expert Judge -- a dominance asymmetry). *** OPEN DECISION FOR JORDAN (Gate B): confirm SIMPLE MAJORITY, or select an alternative. *** Primary attribute Cognition (as Expert Judge, section 3 Argue Pool -- the ONLY per-type canonical fact); panel size + ballot sharpness/noise are [SEED] calibration (Jordan to set), NOT canon. WIRING (additive, no existing behaviour changed): none of the 8 canonical proceedings map to the panel adjudicator, so build_contest(adjudicator='panel') is the only trigger -- it sets the venue win-condition to VoteAtClose via dataclasses.replace, leaving every other venue parameter untouched. PROSE: params/contest.md section Panel Adjudicator (new) + section Pools Argue(Panel) row + social_contest_v30 section 2 Step 1 Panel row + section 3 Argue Pool Panel row + New-editorial-items ED-137 row (P2 -> Resolved). CODE: sim/personal/contest/dictionaries.py (PANEL_AGGREGATION, PANEL_VOTE_SHARPNESS/NOISE [SEED], panel_win_condition(), PANEL_CLOSURE, ADJUDICATORS_TABLE panel.ed137) + wrapper.py build_contest panel branch + MECHANICS panel_deliberation=WIRED. +checks in sim/personal/contest/_kernel_tests.py (part of the +43 Stage-2 block). Filed provisional / needs_jordan:true; the aggregation-rule choice is the open decision above. This is a NEW ED (round-2 contest_rebuild block, ED 1055-1079). [RATIFIED 2026-07-01 (Jordan, Gate B): the aggregation rule is WEIGHTED-BY-STANDING (NOT the prior simple-majority default). Each juror's ballot counts in proportion to its bench-weight = its EXISTING Adjudicator.discipline (institutional rank/rigor on the bench -- NOT the contestant-side Standing primitive, a different concept; the class name Standing is deliberately not reused, avoiding the Persuasion/Piety-style collision). Side A wins iff the summed bench-weight of the A-ballots > half the total bench weight, else draw. No invented per-juror state (discipline is reused); a mixed-weight bench does not merely mirror its highest-weight juror (a low-weight juror crossing can swing a near-even split; the summed-weight threshold, not one dominant vote, decides), so Panel stays non-dominated vs a single Expert Judge -- verified by a seeded kernel test showing a mixed-weight bench goes either way. Implemented: resolver.VoteAtClose gains an aggregation='weighted_by_standing' branch; dictionaries.PANEL_AGGREGATION='weighted_by_standing' + panel_win_condition() carries it. Alternatives simple_majority / unanimity_required recorded, not chosen. status flipped provisional -> ratified / needs_jordan:false.]

description: Contest rebuild Stage 2 / Gate B (round-2 revision, finding 4): the two Obscuring styles (Suppression, Insinuation) are strictly DOMINATED by their Revealing genre-mates (Precedent, Vision) in single-exchange contests. An Obscuring win moves NO Persuasion Track (social_contest_v30 §4:179); it plants a Doubt Marker whose sole effect (-2 to the opponent's NEXT winning exchange, consumed on use, min 0) has EV EXACTLY 0 when there is no next exchange -- so in Casual Dispute (1,1), Personal Appeal (1,1), and Church Tribunal (1,5)/Private Negotiation (1,3) run at length 1, an Obscuring win forgoes the sole track/tally movement for nothing, strictly worse than the Revealing genre-mate at equal successes (churn axiom: every style must be correct somewhere). RESOLUTION (best-grounded, flagged, easy to swap): give the Doubt Marker a non-zero TERMINAL value -- an unconsumed marker still on the board when the contest CLOSES (a single-exchange proceeding, or the final exchange of any proceeding: no 'next winning exchange' remains) is applied ONCE, terminally: its -2 is subtracted from the closing margin/band AGAINST the marked side (i.e. in the Obscuring winner's / planter's favour -- the marker always works against the party it is placed on, exactly as it fires in play: v30:180-181 places it on the opponent and reduces THAT side's winning margin) (min 0; slides a Compromise-zone result one step toward the Obscuring winner, cannot reach a decisive band it does not otherwise reach). This gives the marker a non-zero terminal value so the Obscuring genre-mates stop being strictly worse. [ROUND-3 REVISION, finding 1: corrected the direction wording -- the prior 'in the marked side's favour' phrasing was self-contradictory with the same sentence's 'toward the Obscuring winner' and, read literally, rewarded the Obscuring LOSER, re-introducing the very dominance this ED cures; both clauses now name the Obscuring winner / planter as beneficiary. Wording/direction fix only, not a design change; the (a)-vs-(b) fork remains open for Jordan.] [ROUND-4 REVISION, finding 1 (major): the terminal rule is now SPLIT BY RESOLUTION MECHANISM. The prior single clause was written entirely in banded 'margin / band / Compromise-zone' language -- constructs that exist ONLY on the PersuasionTrack win-condition. But of the four single-exchange-capable proceedings this ED names, only Church Tribunal (tracker_mode='required') is banded; Casual Dispute (tracker_mode='none', ALWAYS), Personal Appeal (tracker_mode='optional', DEFAULT), and Private Negotiation (tracker_mode='optional', DEFAULT) at length 1 resolve by resolver.TallyAtClose, which returns raw A/B/draw with NO band or margin. So the design commitment was well-defined only for Church Tribunal and UNDEFINED for exactly the two proceedings the finding cites as motivating cases (Casual Dispute always, Personal Appeal default), leaving Suppression/Insinuation still potentially dominated THERE. FIX -- specify the terminal marker separately for each mechanism (shared direction: against the marked side, min 0): (i) BANDED (PersuasionTrack): -2 off the closing margin/band, slides a Compromise result one step toward the Obscuring winner (the already-written branch; Church Tribunal). (ii) RAW-TALLY (TallyAtClose): no band exists, so -2 off the MARKED SIDE'S RAW adv before the A/B/draw majority comparison (min 0) -- a close tally can flip toward the Obscuring winner or fall to a draw, but the marker can never manufacture a lead the planter did not earn. Added DOUBT_MARKER_FIELD (banded/tally branches + tally-scoped alternative) and a _doubt_marker_branches_specified() guard requiring BOTH branches present, so the tally subset can never silently go undefined again. Design-table wording fix only, no resolution-path number change; the (a)-vs-(b) fork remains open for Jordan.] *** OPEN DECISION FOR JORDAN: confirm the terminal-value rule (a) -- split banded-vs-tally as above, OR select the alternative (b) -- gate Obscuring style-selection out of single-exchange proceedings entirely (or, tally-scoped, out of just the raw-tally single-exchange proceedings) and document why. *** SCOPE HONESTY: this is a DESIGN-TABLE commitment only -- the resolver does NOT yet consume orientation (Stage-3 CR5 rhetoric-armature scope), so NO resolution-path number changes this pass; the flag records the ratified-pending rule for Stage 3 to implement. Couples to the Suppression/Insinuation FLAVOR honesty (Lens 6): the Obscuring flavor is only behaviorally honest once this is resolved. PROSE: designs/scene/social_contest_v30.md §4 Step 4 (Obscuring-win block, Terminal Doubt bullet -- now split banded (i) / raw-tally (ii)) + params/contest.md §Interaction Types (Terminal Doubt block after the Obscuring-win line, same split). CODE: sim/personal/contest/dictionaries.py DOUBT_MARKER_TERMINAL + DOUBT_MARKER_FIELD (banded/tally/tally_alternative) + DOUBT_MARKER flag (problem/resolution/banded_terminal/tally_terminal/alternative/scope_note/swap point) + _doubt_marker_branches_specified() guard + Suppression/Insinuation flavor comments cross-referencing ED-1060. TESTS: +5 kernel checks (sim/personal/contest/_kernel_tests.py) -- DOUBT_MARKER records the fork + default terminal_value + scope-honest + round-3 direction guard + round-4 BOTH-branches-specified guard (banded and tally terminal behavior both present, tally branch references raw adv, no band-only construct left undefined). Filed provisional / needs_jordan:true. NEW ED (round-2 contest_rebuild block, ED 1055-1079). [RATIFIED 2026-07-01 (Jordan, Gate B): confirmed as ALREADY IMPLEMENTED -- the Terminal Doubt rule is a terminal value everywhere (the -2 at close, applied consistently to BOTH the banded/PersuasionTrack branch and the tally/TallyAtClose branch, split-by-mechanism as the round-4 revision specified). Jordan confirmed the rule as-built; NO code or prose change is needed. status flipped provisional -> ratified / needs_jordan:false.]

description: Contest rebuild Stage 2 / Gate B (FINAL ratification, Jordan): the Guilds either-axis faction boost is CONTEXT-DERIVED FROM THE VENUE, correcting a stale no-GM defect in canon. params/contest.md Faction Boosts and social_contest_v30.md Sec2 Step 3 both said the Guilds boost is 'GM picks' (params) / 'GM picks one' (v30) -- a genuine defect under the no-GM mandate (CLAUDE.md: 'there is no GM -- the engine resolves everything'). CANON-LEADS-CODE-FOLLOWS: the PROSE was corrected FIRST (both files) to describe the actual engine rule, then dictionaries.py implemented to match. THE RATIFIED RULE (Jordan already chose context-derived-from-venue, not GM/orator/random pick): the Guilds boost applies to whichever axis -- a genre OR an orientation -- the CURRENT contest's ADJUDICATOR already favours, derived from the adjudicator's ethos/pathos/logos weighting via the corpus Aristotle mapping already present (Expert Judge / Panel <-> logos / Cognition, Crowd <-> pathos / Charisma, No Adjudicator <-> ethos / Attunement -- reused, no new venue field invented). The adjudicator's DOMINANT appeal maps to the boosted value: logos -> Memory (forensic/past genre; RhetoricalWeights logos_past home + Genre 'Retention (has been)'=past), pathos -> Projection (deliberative/future genre; pathos_future home + Genre 'Protention (yet to be)'=future), ethos -> Revealing (the ethos-bearing orientation, per the Precedent style 'Revealing/ethos-bearing argument raises Face'). Deterministic: an exact tie in the adjudicator's char weights breaks by the Aristotelian order (logos > pathos > ethos); no randomness. Context-driven, not fixed: a custom pathos-leaning judge derives Projection even though it is an 'expert_judge'-type object (reads the adjudicator.character() signal, which a Panel averages over its members). PROSE: params/contest.md Faction Boosts (Guilds row 'GM picks' -> 'Venue-derived (engine)' + a new resolved-by-the-engine paragraph + ED-1061 comment) + social_contest_v30.md Sec2 Step 3 (Guilds row 'GM picks one' -> 'Venue-derived (engine)' + a resolved-by-the-engine paragraph + ED-1061 comment). CODE: sim/personal/contest/dictionaries.py FACTION_BOOSTS['guilds'].boost='Context (venue-derived)' (was 'GM picks') + _GUILDS_APPEAL_TO_BOOST mapping + guilds_boost_for(adjudicator) derivation. TESTS: +7 kernel checks (sim/personal/contest/_kernel_tests.py) -- Guilds row no longer 'GM picks'; Expert Judge->Memory, Crowd->Projection, No Adjudicator->Revealing, Panel->Memory; context-driven (pathos-leaning judge->Projection); deterministic neutral tie-break->logos/Memory. Filed RATIFIED / needs_jordan:false from the start (Jordan already chose context-derived-from-venue). NEW ED (round-2 contest_rebuild block, ED 1055-1079).
